## Phased Implementation Plan

**Platform order:** aarch64 first, x86_64 second.

### Phase 0: Architecture Documentation (complete)

Full documentation and project scaffolding so future contributors have complete context.

**Deliverables:** `docs/` directory with architecture, IPC, syscall catalog, servers, boot, drivers, musl, memory layout, build, and MINIX 3 mapping documentation. Cargo workspace with all crate placeholders. CLAUDE.md, LICENSE (BSD-3-Clause).

**Milestone:** `docs/` is comprehensive enough to start coding Phase 1 without re-exploring MINIX 3.

### Phase 1: Kernel Scaffolding + Boot (aarch64, QEMU virt) (complete)

- Cargo workspace with `kernel`, `kernel-shared` crates
- Builds via `aarch64-unknown-none` + linker script (`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/linker.ld`); the bespoke `aarch64-minix-kernel.json` target spec is deferred to Phase 2 (the stock target is sufficient for boot)
- Vendor Limine v9.x binary + header (`external/limine/Makefile`); Rust-side request block in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/limine.rs` (base revision, HHDM, memmap, paging mode, stack size)
- PL011 UART driver at `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/uart.rs` with `core::fmt::Write` adapter; MMIO base is HHDM-relative (Limine base revision 2 keeps the [0, 4 GiB) blanket map covering PL011)
- aarch64 exception vector table (`vectors.S` + `exception.rs`); any unexpected trap routes through `exception_entry` and panics with a decoded ESR_EL1/ELR_EL1/FAR_EL1 dump
- `tools/qemu-run.sh` is the cargo runner: stages an ESP under `target/esp/`, auto-detects edk2 firmware, boots with `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt` via `-drive file=fat:rw:...`
- **Milestone:** `cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release` boots through UEFI + Limine, prints `minix.rs booting on aarch64` + HHDM offset, then halts in `wfe`. Exception path verified by injecting a deliberate fault and observing a clean panic dump.

### Phase 2: Kernel IPC + Scheduling (the heart of MINIX)

Phase 2 is split into 6 PR-sized slices. Each slice is independently
buildable, boots, and produces observable output. The Phase 2 milestone
("two processes exchange IPC messages") is satisfied at the end of Slice
2.5; Slice 2.6 finishes the kernel-call surface needed by Phase 3.

- **Slice 2.1** ✓ shipped (PR #3, merged 2026-05-20) — `kernel-shared`
  foundation: `Message` (104-byte, 8-aligned `repr(C)`), `Endpoint`
  (generation-aware, 15-bit signed proc field, sentinels derived from
  `ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP`), IPC primitive numbers, kernel-call numbers,
  task and server endpoint constants (renumbered contiguously; no
  static `LOG` slot), MINIX errno values. `NR_PROCS = 1024`. 21
  host-side unit tests; no kernel changes.
- **Slice 2.2** ✓ shipped (PR #4, merged 2026-05-22) — `Proc` and `Priv` structs with kernel-internal
  `RTS_*` / `MF_*` / priv-flag / trap-mask constants in
  `kernel/src/proc/flags.rs`; static `PROC_TABLE` (1029 slots) and
  `PRIV_TABLE` (64 slots) under `UnsafeCell` + `unsafe impl Sync` with
  documented single-threaded boot invariants; a 16-entry boot `IMAGE`
  drives `proc::init()`, and `proc::dump_tables()` writes a tabular
  UART view (5 kernel tasks runnable, 11 boot servers blocked on
  `RTS_NO_PRIV`, contiguous priv-ids 0–15). `kernel-shared` migrated
  `ProcNr` / `PrivId` / `SysId` from `i32` aliases to newtypes,
  added `NR_SYS_CALLS = 32` and a new `sys_limits` module
  (`NR_IO_RANGE`, `NR_IRQ`, `NR_MEM_RANGE`). `ArchRegisterFrame` stub
  in `arch/aarch64/context.rs` ready for slice 2.3 to populate. 25
  host-side tests pass (21 existing + 4 new newtype round-trips);
  clean release build with no warnings.
- **Slice 2.3** ✓ shipped (PR #5, merged 2026-05-22) — aarch64 SVC entry
  cooperative context switch. `entry.S`
  promotes the kernel from Limine's EL1t to EL1h on a primed SP_EL1
  (`mov x9, sp; msr SPSel, #1; mov sp, x9` — the only sequence that
  worked on QEMU virt / Cortex-A72; a bare `msr sp_el1, xN` from EL1t
  silently locks the core). New `arch/aarch64/{mmu,userland,trap,
  user_stub}.S/rs` build a minimal TTBR0_EL1 walk that maps one EL0
  code page (RO+X) and one EL0 stack page (RW+NX) at low VAs, copies a
  hand-coded `svc #0; b .` stub blob into the code page, and erets into
  EL0. Vector slot 8 (`el0_64_sync`) is specialized to `b
  el0_64_sync_entry` in `trap.S`; the SVC handler parks `&mut p.regs`
  in `TPIDR_EL1` so it can save state directly into the proc table and
  tail-call back to EL0 via `el1_return_to_user`. `do_ipc(frame)` in
  the new `kernel/src/ipc/` module prints each call and stores `OK` in
  `frame.x[0]`. `proc::sched::switch_to_user` wraps the eret. Verified
  in QEMU: `do_ipc[N]: call_nr=3 src_dst=32766 msg=0x0...` repeats
  ~16K times per second.
- **Slice 2.4** ✓ shipped (PR #6, merged 2026-05-23) — GICv3 + ARM generic timer + run queues. New
  `arch/aarch64/{gic,timer,irq}.rs` + `interrupt.S` bring up GICD/GICR
  (QEMU virt cortex-a72, `-machine virt,gic-version=3`), enable PPI 27
  (EL1 virtual timer) at 100 Hz, and route the IRQ through vector slot
  9 → `el0_64_irq_entry` (mirrors slice 2.3's slot-8 treatment) →
  `do_irq` → ICC_IAR1_EL1 ack / `clock::tick` / ICC_EOIR1_EL1. New
  `proc::sched` adds priority-banded FIFO run queues (`enqueue`,
  `dequeue`, `pick_proc`, `reschedule`, `run`) chained through
  `Proc::next_ready: Option<ProcNr>`. New `kernel/src/clock.rs` owns
  the per-tick handler — prints `current_proc.name[0]` to PL011, then
  decrements `quantum_left` and triggers `reschedule` on
  `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`. `userland_bootstrap` now stages two EL0 stubs (A
  and B at `0x40_0000`/`0x41_0000` sharing one code page, distinct
  stacks at `0x80_0000`/`0x81_0000`); SPSR drops to `0x340` so IRQs
  are unmasked at EL0. Verified in QEMU: 171 A ticks vs 170 B ticks
  over ~3.4 s, clean `AAAAA BBBBB AAAAA …` 5-per-quantum bursts with
  `do_ipc[N]` SVC traces interleaved (proves SVC + IRQ paths coexist).
- **Slice 2.5** ✓ shipped (PR #7, merged 2026-05-23) — IPC primitives in
  Rust. New `kernel/src/ipc/{mod,
  message, send, receive, notify, senda, deadlock}.rs`: `do_ipc`
  dispatcher with trap-mask gating, `mini_send` / `mini_receive` /
  `mini_notify` / `mini_sendnb` faithful to MINIX 3 `kernel/proc.c`,
  `deadlock_check` (size-2 SEND↔RECV legalization via the `function<<2`
  trick), `mini_senda` stub returning `ENOSYS` (deferred to a later
  slice; no Phase-2 consumer). New `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset`
  capture `nr` and end the borrow before calling `enqueue`/`dequeue`
  so RTS transitions stay run-queue-coherent. New `sched::schedule_next`
  picks the highest-priority runnable proc, parks its frame in
  `TPIDR_EL1`, and flushes any pending `MF_DELIVERMSG` into the user
  buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` — invoked from the SVC tail via the
  new `el1_svc_tail` shim (`trap.S` now reads `bl do_ipc; bl
  el1_svc_tail; b el1_return_to_user`) and from `reschedule` + `run`.
  `Proc` gains `deliver_msg_vir: u64`. User-memory IPC reads via
  `core::ptr::read_volatile` with a coarse `USER_VA_TOP = 1 << 48`
  bounds check; fault recovery is Phase 3. `user_stub.S` rewritten:
  two PC-relative-free blobs in separate sections — stub A
  (`SENDREC` to endpoint B, persistent counter in `x19`), stub B
  (`RECEIVE` from `ANY` then `SEND`-reply to `m_source`). `userland.rs`
  installs two physical code pages, four `map_4k` calls, plus
  `install_stub_privs` filling priv slots 16/17 with
  `trap_mask = SRV_T` and `ipc_to` cross-targeting A↔B. Verified in
  QEMU over 8 s: ~897K IPC ops; ~2990 each of `call=1`/`2`/`3` with
  every line `result=0`; no panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`; `A`/`B`
  clock-tick interleaving from slice 2.4 still visible. **Phase 2
  milestone reached.**
- **Slice 2.6** ✓ shipped (PR #8, merged 2026-05-25) — Kernel-call
  dispatch + minimum `SYS_*` set. New
  `kernel/src/system/{mod,do_getinfo,stubs}.rs` implement the MINIX 3
  fast-path shape: `ipc::dispatch`'s SENDREC arm detects
  `src_dst_e == boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)` and diverts to
  `system::kernel_call_sendrec`, which runs synchronously in the
  caller's EL1 context (mirrors `kernel/system.c::kernel_call`). The
  dispatcher applies `Priv::k_call_mask` gating and the same `ipc_to`
  permission check that `mini_send` does (re-exported `get_sys_bit`).
  `SYS_GETINFO` is real (`GET_WHOAMI` writes caller's endpoint,
  priv flags, and `name` into the payload in-place and returns `OK`);
  the other 13 Phase-2 `SYS_*` calls land as `ENOSYS` stubs with their
  canonical MINIX 3 `do_*` names so Phase 3+ can swap real handlers in
  without touching dispatch. A `const _: () = assert!(NR_KERN_CALLS_
  PHASE2 == 14)` next to the dispatch match locks arm coverage — adding
  a new `SYS_*` without a new arm is a compile error.
  `kernel-shared/callnr.rs` gains `GET_WHOAMI = 12` (matches MINIX 3
  `include/minix/sysinfo.h`) and `SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN = 16` (kernel's
  `PROC_NAME_LEN`; deviates from MINIX 3's 44 B because minix.rs never
  stores more than 16 B per slot). `user_stub.S` gains a third
  `.rodata.user_stub_c` blob — SENDREC to `ENDPOINT_SYS` (`0x7FFE` =
  `boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)`) with `m_type = SYS_GETINFO` and
  `payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI`, persistent counter in `x19`.
  `userland.rs` adds stub C's code/stack pages plus `STUB_C_PROC_NR`
  (13) / `STUB_C_PRIV_ID` (18). `install_stub_c_priv` differs from the
  slice-2.5 helper: `trap_mask = USR_T` (only SENDREC), `ipc_to`
  opened only to SYSTEM's priv slot (resolved at boot via
  `proc_table_ref()` so the IMAGE order isn't hard-coded), and
  `k_call_mask` opened only to `SYS_GETINFO`. `ipc/mod.rs`'s trace
  gains a `TRACE_HEAD = 12` head carve-out — C's fast-path rate
  (~125 K ops/sec) would otherwise drown A↔B's ~10 SVCs/sec in the
  modulo-100 sampling and the slice-2.5 ping-pong would look like it
  regressed; the head trace shows each stub's first SVC explicitly.
  Verified in QEMU over 8 s: SVC #1 = stub A SENDREC → B (result=0),
  SVCs #2–4 = stub B RECEIVE/SEND/RECEIVE (all result=0), SVCs #5+ =
  stub C SENDREC → SYSTEM with 6536 `[ksys N]` dispatches all
  `result=0`; clean — no `el0_sync_unexpected`, no panic.

Aggregate scope:

- `kernel-shared`: Message struct, Endpoint, all call numbers, error codes
- Process table (`Proc`), privilege table (`Priv`)
- IPC: `mini_send`, `mini_receive`, `mini_notify`, `deadlock_check`
- aarch64 arch: exception vectors, GICv3, ARM generic timer, SVC entry, context switch
- Run queues: `enqueue`, `dequeue`, `pick_proc`, `switch_to_user`
- Kernel calls: minimum set (SYS_GETINFO, SYS_PRIVCTL, SYS_FORK, SYS_EXEC, SYS_EXIT, SYS_COPY, SYS_SAFECOPY, SYS_IRQCTL, SYS_VMCTL, SYS_SCHEDULE, SYS_SETALARM, SYS_TIMES, SYS_DIAGCTL, SYS_SETGRANT)
- Boot image unpacking, load two test processes
- **Milestone:** Two processes exchange IPC messages ("ping-pong test")

### Phase 3: VM Server + Memory Management

Phase 3 is split into 7 PR-sized slices (decomposition tracked in
`~/.claude/plans/work-on-phase-3-optimized-petal.md`). Each slice
independently builds, boots, and prints observable progress. The Phase 3
milestone ("Boot processes each have isolated address spaces; VM handles
page faults") is satisfied at the end of slice 3.4; slices 3.5/3.6 then
add brk + mmap on top. POSIX fork and exec are deferred to Phase 4
(PM-driven).

Architecture choices (locked in by plan): per-process TTBR0 + 8-bit
ARMv8 ASIDs, kernel writes all user PTEs (VM passes decisions in via
SYS_VMCTL subcalls), kernel reads cross-AS user memory via HHDM after
walking the target proc's page table, VM uses static `[Region; N]`
per-proc tables (no allocator), stubs A/B/C from Phase 2.5/2.6 migrated
to per-proc TTBR0 in 3.1b and kept as regression coverage.

- **Slice 3.1a** ✓ shipped (PR #9, merged 2026-05-27) — Physical frame allocator + addrspace API, kernel-only,
  no EL0 changes. New `kernel/src/mm/{mod,frame}.rs`: intrusive free-list +
  per-region bump pointers seeded from Limine `MEMMAP_USABLE` entries
  (capacity `MAX_REGIONS = 16`; QEMU virt + Apple Silicon QEMU both fit
  comfortably). Frames inside the kernel image, embedded boot image, and
  Phase-2.5/2.6 static stub pages live in `EXECUTABLE_AND_MODULES` and
  are never visible to the allocator — no explicit reservation logic
  needed. `alloc_frame` zeros on hand-out so the caller never sees
  residual state; `free_frame` pushes via HHDM. `kernel-shared` /
  `Limine` integration: extended `arch/aarch64/limine.rs` with a
  `MemmapEntry` repr-C struct and a `memmap_entries()` iterator that
  walks the `**entry` indirection Limine uses. New
  `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs`: `AddrSpace::new` allocates
  one L0 frame; `map_page(va, pa, Prot)` walks/allocates L1/L2/L3 on
  demand via the frame allocator, writes the leaf PTE through HHDM
  using the same PTE bit constants as `mmu.rs`; `walk_pt(va)` returns
  `Option<u64>`; `destroy()` recursively frees intermediate tables and
  the L0 root (leaf frames are caller-owned, not freed here). One-shot
  `mm_smoke_test` in `kmain` builds a throwaway AddrSpace, installs four
  mappings across distinct L2 slots, walks them all (plus one negative
  check), tears down, then verifies the free-list is LIFO by asserting
  the next `alloc_frame` returns the just-freed L0 PA. The smoke test is
  removed in 3.1b once real per-proc AddrSpaces replace `userland.rs`'s
  static `L0/L1/L2/L3_*` tables. Verified in QEMU over 8 s:
  `[mm] frame_alloc OK ttbr0_pa=0x40000000 / map OK / walk OK / free OK`
  prints in order; A↔B ping-pong head trace (`[ipc 1..4]`) and stub C
  SYS_GETINFO carve-out (~726 K SVCs, every line `result=0`) both
  unchanged from slice 2.6; no panic, no `el0_sync_unexpected`.
- **Slice 3.1b** ✓ shipped (PR #10, merged 2026-05-27) — Per-process
  TTBR0s + 8-bit ASIDs + minimal
  page-fault-diagnostic handler. `Proc` gains `ttbr0_pa: u64` and
  `asid: u8` (placed in a new "MMU state" block between `deliver_msg_vir`
  and `next_ready`; `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes both — kernel tasks and
  RTS_NO_PRIV boot servers keep the sentinel). New
  `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/asid.rs` carries an `UnsafeCell<u8>` counter
  starting at `FIRST_ASID = 1` (0 reserved for "uninitialized"), with
  `alloc_asid()` panicking on 8-bit wrap — real rollover deferred to
  Phase 4 since slice 3.1b only hands out three. `mmu.rs` loses the
  slice-2.3 monolithic `activate_user_ttbr0` (plus the slice-2.5 static

  all unused since 3.1a's `AddrSpace` took over) and gains three new

  without binding any TTBR0; `switch_ttbr0_with_asid(ttbr0_pa, asid)`
  writes `TTBR0_EL1 = ttbr0_pa | ((asid as u64) << 48)` then issues
  `isb / tlbi aside1, Xt / dsb ish / isb` — TLBI is ASID-tagged and
  unconditional (the simpler control flow beats micro-optimizing three
  ASIDs). `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` is rewritten end-to-end:
  every static `L0_TABLE` / `L1_TABLE` / `L2_TABLE` / `L3_CODE_TABLE` /
  `L3_STACK_TABLE` / `USER_CODE_PAGE_*` / `USER_STACK_PAGE_*` and the
  `kernel_pa_of` helper are gone. Each stub's `build_stub` allocates an
  `AddrSpace::new()` L0 root, a code frame (stub blob copied in via
  `mm::phys_to_hhdm` + `mmu::flush_icache_range`), and a stack frame

  proc slot by an 8-arg `populate_stub_slot`. The `AddrSpace` value is
  `core::mem::forget`-ed since the page-table tree is now durably owned
  via `Proc::ttbr0_pa`; only exit/exec paths in later slices will
  `destroy`. `proc::sched::schedule_next` adds two lines between

  && asid != 0)` (kernel tasks would silently inherit the previous
  TTBR0 otherwise) and a `switch_ttbr0_with_asid` call. The order
  matters — the message flush writes via the active TTBR0, so the new
  proc's AS must be live first; cross-AS IPC delivery is still slice
  3.4's job. `el0_sync_unexpected` in `arch/aarch64/exception.rs`

  prints IFSC + the `fsc_name` mnemonic; EC=0x24 prints DFSC + WnR +

  el1_svc_tail; b el1_return_to_user`) so the unblocked proc is rescheduled

- **Slice 3.3** ✓ shipped (PR #12, merged 2026-06-01) — Real `SYS_VMCTL`

- **Slice 3.5** ✓ shipped (PR #15, merged 2026-06-08) — VM
  region tracking + `VM_BRK`. New `servers/vm/src/region.rs`: a static

  marks it `Unused`, and returns the page-aligned `[start, min(end, region.end))`
  sweep range — the `min` cap stops an overstated `len` from freeing heap

761	  frames. Ten new host tests cover both. The VM server (`main.rs`) dispatches
762	  `VM_MMAP → handle_mmap` (reply chosen base in payload `0..8`) and
763	  `VM_MUNMAP → handle_munmap`, which loops `SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_UNMAP)` over the
764	  returned range, ignoring the harmless `EINVAL` a never-faulted page returns.
765	  No kernel dispatch, `do_vmctl`, or priv-wiring changes — mmap/munmap ride the
766	  same D→VM SENDREC edge `install_stub_d_priv` already opened for brk.
767	  `user_stub.S` extends stub D after its brk sequence: `VM_MMAP(0x2000)` →
768	  stash the returned base in callee-saved `x22` → touch it (faults once, VM
769	  maps it) → `VM_MUNMAP(x22, 0x2000)` → steady loop over the two heap pages only
770	  (the mmap page is now unmapped). Docs: a real mdBook *Memory Management*
771	  chapter (`book/src/memory/overview.md`) written from source — frame
772	  allocator, per-proc `AddrSpace`/TTBR0/ASID, the page-fault → VM flow,
773	  `SYS_VMCTL`, and brk/mmap/munmap — plus CLAUDE.md notes on VM region kinds and
774	  the `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP`-on-hole behavior. Verified in QEMU over 8 s: five `[as]`
775	  lines (A/B/C/D + vm); three `[pf] proc=D` faults (`far=0x1000000`,
776	  `0x1004000`, `0x2000000`); three `[ksys VMCTL_PT_MAP] proc=D` (the two heap
777	  pages + `va=0x2000000`) and exactly one `[ksys VMCTL_PT_UNMAP] proc=D
778	  va=0x2000000` (the second, never-touched mmap page is a silent kernel
779	  `EINVAL`); D then round-robins with no re-fault; A↔B ping-pong + C

781	
782	Aggregate scope (Phase 3 as a whole):
783	
784	- `kernel/src/mm/`: physical frame allocator
785	- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs`: per-process page-table API
786	- Per-proc TTBR0 + ASID allocator; context switch updates TTBR0_EL1

788	- `kernel/src/system/do_vmctl.rs`: real SYS_VMCTL subcalls

791	  brk, mmap (all static-allocation; no heap allocator in VM)
792	- **Milestone:** Boot processes each have isolated address spaces; VM

795	### Phase 4: Core Servers (PM, VFS, RS, DS, SCHED) + init
796	
797	Phase 4 is split into 8 PR-sized slices (decomposition tracked in
798	`~/.claude/plans/go-ahead-with-phase-dapper-rain.md`). Each slice independently
799	builds, boots, and prints observable progress — same cadence as Phases 2–3. The

804	filesystem/musl until Phase 5, so `SYS_EXEC` loads ELF binaries packed into the
805	boot-image archive — the same archive that loads the servers — and Phase 5 later

809	and on quantum exhaustion the kernel either requeues or notifies the proc's

816	  `server-rt` SEF framework + migrate VM onto it + finish

861	- **Slice 4.3** ✓ shipped (PR #25, merged 2026-06-27) — Real
862	  user-space scheduling: kernel delegatable scheduler + SCHED server (single PR).
863	  `Proc` gains `scheduler: Endpoint` (`NONE` = kernel-scheduled, the boot
864	  default; `populate_proc` sets it and `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes it to `NONE`).
865	  `sched::reschedule` branches on it: `NONE` keeps the slice-2.4 refill+rotate;
866	  otherwise it dequeues the preempted proc (which `clock::tick`'s bare `fetch_or`
867	  left enqueued), leaves `RTS_NO_QUANTUM` set, and sends `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`
868	  to the scheduler via new `ipc::send::mini_sched_no_quantum_send` (a near-clone
869	  of 3.4's `mini_pf_send`, wrapped by `ipc::send_no_quantum` which materializes
870	  the proc-table slice like `send_pagefault_to_vm`). `SYS_SCHEDULE` becomes real
871	  and a new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` lands (`kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs`), both
872	  target-taking and routed beside `SYS_VMCTL` in `kernel_call_dispatch`:
873	  `do_schedule` sets a target's priority/quantum and re-admits it
874	  (`rts_unset(RTS_NO_QUANTUM)` if off-queue, else dequeue+enqueue for the band
875	  move); `do_schedctl` claims (`scheduler = caller`) or releases
876	  (`SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `NONE`) a target. `kernel-shared/callnr.rs` gains
877	  `SYS_SCHEDCTL`, `SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL`, `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 = 15` (one-slice
878	  `_PHASE3` alias, dropped in 4.4), and a `SCHED_RQ_BASE = 0xF00` range
879	  (`SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`/`START`/`STOP`/`SET_NICE`, `NR_SCHED_MSGS = 4`,
880	  const-asserted distinct from VM/SEF/DS and below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`) + host
881	  tests; `init_boot_image`'s `k_call_mask` fill widens to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4`
882	  so SCHED may issue the two calls. SCHED (`servers/sched`, server-rt based):
883	  SEF loop publishing to DS, handlers for all four `SCHEDULING_*`; the policy is
884	  a round-robin quantum refresh at a fixed managed band (`USER_Q = 8`, the
885	  boot-server band, so a CPU-bound managed proc round-robins instead of sinking
886	  behind the kernel-scheduled stubs) held in a static `[SchedProc; 16]`
887	  `UnsafeCell` table (`servers/sched/src/policy.rs`, pure helpers host-tested
888	  like `ds/registry.rs`). MINIX-style priority aging (drop a band + periodic
889	  `balance_queues` boost) is deferred to 4.4's `SYS_SETALARM` — without the boost,
890	  dropping a band would starve the managed proc behind the band-8 stubs.
891	  `SCHEDULING_START`/`STOP`/`SET_NICE` are implemented for PM/RS to drive from
892	  4.5+; in 4.3 the kernel pre-delegates stub C (`userland.rs` sets its
893	  `scheduler = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)`), the live exercise. Added to the
894	  MXBI `servers` array in `build.rs` (proc_nr 9). Verified in QEMU over 10 s:
895	  eight `[as]` lines (vm/ds/vfs/sched asid 1–4, stubs A–D asid 5–8); SCHED boots
896	  through SEF (`[ipc 10/11] caller=9` GET_WHOAMI + DS publish); head traces show
897	  the round-trip — `[noq N] proc=C nr=13 -> scheduler=0x9` (kernel delegates) and
898	  `[ksys SYS_SCHEDULE] target=C nr=13 prio=8 quantum=5 result=0` (SCHED
899	  re-admits); stub C sustains (`[ksys]` `caller=13` to sample 250 800), A↔B
900	  ping-pong + D's three `[pf]` (brk×2 + mmap) resolved by VM all intact; every
901	  line `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host: `cargo test
902	  -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-sched -p minixrs-server-rt` green; `cargo
903	  check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean.
904	- **Slice 4.4** ✓ shipped (PR #26, merged 2026-06-27) —
905	  RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`. `SYS_SETALARM` replaces its
906	  slice-2.6 `ENOSYS` stub with a per-proc one-shot timer: `Proc` gains
907	  `alarm_at: u64` (absolute uptime tick, 0 = disarmed; `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes it),
908	  and `kernel/src/system/do_setalarm.rs` (caller-local, like the other
909	  non-target `SYS_*`) reads a relative `delta` (u64, payload `0..8`), stores
910	  `alarm_at = uptime()+delta` (disarms on `delta==0`), and replies the previous
911	  timer's remaining ticks. `clock.rs` gains an `EARLIEST_ALARM` fast-path gate
912	  (`arm_alarm`/`set_earliest_alarm`) so `tick()` stays O(1) and only pays the
913	  O(N) scan when an alarm is actually due; the scan + delivery live in
914	  `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` (a kernel-originated-delivery wrapper beside
915	  `send_pagefault_to_vm`/`send_no_quantum`), which clears each expired
916	  `alarm_at`, calls `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm`, traces `[alarm N]` (head
917	  carve-out + modulo, like `TRACE_HEAD`), and recomputes the next-earliest.
918	  `deliver_alarm` is a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK` with **no `ipc_to`
919	  check** (CLOCK's bitmap is empty, so routing through `mini_notify` would deny
920	  it) — immediate when the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked, else deferred via
921	  `notify_pending` against CLOCK's priv slot (drained by `mini_receive`). No new
922	  kernel-shared constants — the alarm reuses `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` + `CLOCK`, and
923	  `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 15. RS (`servers/rs`, server-rt based, already
924	  `ROOT_SYS_PROC` + `sig_mgr` with full priv wiring from `init_boot_image`) boots
925	  through SEF, publishes to DS, arms a periodic alarm (`ALARM_PERIOD = 100`
926	  ticks), and on each fire pings a static peer set (DS/VM/SCHED/VFS via
927	  `boot_endpoint`) with `ipc_notify`, tallying acks in a host-tested

929	  restart-on-crash is detect-only (the `monitor::sweep` dead count — EL0 can't
930	  log and exec is a later slice). The RS heartbeat reuses the existing SEF ping
931	  (peers ack via `server-rt`'s `sef.receive`); the alarm `NOTIFY` from CLOCK is
932	  classified `Application` (source ≠ RS), so RS's loop keys on
933	  `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)`. Added to the MXBI `servers` array in
934	  `build.rs` (proc_nr 2); no new boot priv wiring. Verified in QEMU over 25 s:
935	  nine `[as]` lines (vm/ds/vfs/sched/rs asid 1–5, stubs A–D asid 6–9); head
936	  `[ipc]` shows RS GET_WHOAMI + DS publish (`[ipc 3/4]`), DS reply to RS
937	  (`[ipc 10]`), and RS's first heartbeat pings to DS/VM (`[ipc 11/12]`); six
938	  periodic `[alarm N] owner=r nr=2 at=100..600` fires; stub D's three `[pf]` →
939	  VM, SCHED `[noq]` delegation, and A↔B ping-pong + C `SYS_GETINFO` all intact;
940	  every line `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host: `cargo test
941	  -p minixrs-rs -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-server-rt` green; `cargo
942	  check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean.
943	- **Slice 4.5** ✓ shipped (PR #27, merged 2026-07-17) —
944	  PM part A: mproc + getpid + real `SYS_PRIVCTL` + minimal signals
945	  (kernel-mediated, MINIX-faithful). The kernel gains the signal trio
946	  `SYS_KILL`/`SYS_GETKSIG`/`SYS_ENDKSIG` (`0x60F..0x611`;
947	  `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` 15 → 18, overdue `_PHASE3` alias dropped) in
948	  `kernel/src/system/do_sig.rs`: `do_kill` validates and calls `cause_sig`,
949	  which records the signal in a new per-proc bitmap (`Proc::sig_pending`),
950	  sets `RTS_SIGNALED | RTS_SIG_PENDING` (the reserved flags, first real use),
951	  and wakes PM with a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `SYSTEM`
952	  (`ipc::notify::deliver_ksig`, a `deliver_alarm` clone — SYSTEM's `ipc_to` is
953	  empty so `mini_notify` would deny it; `do_kill`'s deferred-notify write is
954	  why `kernel_call_dispatch`'s priv param went `&mut`). PM drains with
955	  `SYS_GETKSIG` (hands off the bitmap — the scan requires `sig_pending != 0`,
956	  not just the RTS bit, so a handed-off proc isn't re-returned — and keeps RTS
957	  state) and acknowledges with `SYS_ENDKSIG` (clears it; PM must ENDKSIG
958	  *every* returned endpoint, after any terminate). Terminate itself is
959	  `SYS_EXIT`-lite (`do_exit.rs`, target-taking): dequeue via
960	  `rts_set(RTS_PROC_STOP)`, `alarm_at = 0`, and a `caller_q` unlink of the one
961	  chain named by `sendto_e` when the target died SENDING — AS teardown / slot
962	  free / generation bump stay with 4.6. `SYS_PRIVCTL` becomes real
963	  (`do_privctl.rs`): sole subcode `PRIVCTL_SET_USER` points a
964	  `RTS_NO_PRIV`-frozen target at the new shared USER priv slot
965	  (`table::USER_PRIV_ID` = 20: `USR_T`, `ipc_to` = {PM}, empty `k_call_mask`,
966	  `sig_mgr` = PM; `populate_user_priv` also opens the reverse PM → 20 edge,
967	  `install_stub_d_priv` pattern) and releases it (`EPERM` on a live target —
968	  the freeze gate doubles as authorization). PM (`servers/pm`, MXBI row 6,
969	  proc 0) boots through SEF, publishes to DS, seeds a host-tested
970	  `mproc.rs` table (pids: PM 0, INIT 1, servers 2..10 slot-order parented to

972	  `STUB_A..E_PROC_NR`), releases the new frozen stub E, serves `PM_GETPID`
973	  (new `PM_RQ_BASE = 0x700` — the last free block below `VM_RQ_BASE`; reply
974	  `m_type` *is* the pid, ppid in payload `0..4`), and on `NOTIFY` from
975	  `SYSTEM` runs the drain with default-terminate for user procs
976	  (`MF_PRIV_PROC` servers are skipped — sig2mess waits for RS restarts).
977	  Signal numbers live in new `kernel-shared/src/signal.rs` (POSIX values).
978	  Live demo: stub E is built *frozen* (`build_stub` gains `Option<PrivId>` +
979	  `frozen`; no priv slot, not enqueued) and PM's init unfreezes it into a
980	  `SENDREC PM_GETPID` loop; stub D, after 32 steady-loop iterations, touches
981	  its munmapped mmap page — VM's out-of-region arm now raises
982	  `SYS_KILL(faulter, SIGSEGV)` instead of the slice-3.5 silent return. RS
983	  heartbeats PM as a fifth peer; `ipc::TRACE_HEAD` widened 12 → 24 (six
984	  servers' boot chatter). Verified in QEMU over 25 s: eleven `[as]` lines
985	  (vm/ds/vfs/sched/rs/pm asid 1–6, stubs A–E asid 7–11);
986	  `[ksys SYS_PRIVCTL] target=E nr=15 subcode=1 result=0` exactly once; E's
987	  getpid SENDRECs recur in sampled `[ipc]` (`caller=15 … target=0x0`); stub
988	  D's three resolved `[pf]` + one fatal at the munmapped `far=0x2000000`,
989	  then the full chain in order — `[ksys SYS_KILL] target=D sig=11` →
990	  `[ksys SYS_GETKSIG] target=D map=0x800` → `[ksys SYS_EXIT] target=D` →
991	  `[ksys SYS_ENDKSIG] target=D` — and D goes silent; A↔B ping-pong, C
992	  `[noq]`/`SYS_SCHEDULE` delegation, six periodic RS `[alarm]` fires all
993	  intact; every traced `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host:
994	  `cargo test --workspace` green (new mproc + callnr/signal/com + classify
995	  tests); `cargo check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean; miri
996	  job gains `-p minixrs-pm` (advisory).
997	- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait (two PRs like 3.4). **4.6a**
998	  ✓ shipped (PR #28, merged 2026-07-18): the kernel half — `SYS_FORK` (free
999	  slot, copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by
1000	  walking the parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred;
1001	  zeroes `sig_pending` on slot reuse), completion of 4.5's `SYS_EXIT`-lite into a
1002	  full teardown (`AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot, bump generation,
1003	  `unblock_dependents`), `okendpt` stale-generation rejection, and ASID
1004	  free-list recycling. **4.6b** ◀ ready (branch
1005	  `feature/phase-4-6b-pm-fork-exit-wait`, pending merge): the PM/VM/stub-E half.
1006	  New PM requests `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) let a user
1007	  proc drive the lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape: user → PM, never
1008	  user → kernel). `handle_fork` owns the tree — allocate a child `mproc` slot
1009	  (fork pool `[16, NR_MPROCS)`, slot index = child kernel proc-nr), `SYS_FORK`
1010	  (kernel clones the *frozen* child), `VM_FORK` (new `0xC04` request; VM's
1011	  `region::fork` clones the parent's `ClientRegions`, `MAX_CLIENTS` widened
1012	  16→32 to address the fork pool), `SCHEDULING_START` (SCHED schedules the
1013	  still-frozen child — verified safe: `rts_unset` only enqueues on the
1014	  last-block-bit clear, so `SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_PRIVCTL` leave it a blocked
1015	  `RTS_RECEIVING` receiver), `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` to release the
1016	  freeze, then a reply to **both** halves of the shared SENDREC (child gets
1017	  `m_type = 0`, parent gets the child pid — MINIX fork-returns-twice). `mproc`
1018	  gains a generation-aware `endpoint` field, `exit_status`, `MF_WAITING`, and a
1019	  free-slot allocator / zombie-and-reap helpers (all pure `*_in`, host-tested).
1020	  `handle_exit` = `SCHEDULING_STOP` (before teardown, while the endpoint is
1021	  valid) + `SYS_EXIT` + zombie; `handle_wait` reaps a zombie or suspends the
1022	  parent (no reply) until `handle_exit` wakes it. **Scope decision:** parent
1023	  notification is the zombie + wait-reap handshake only — the kernel signal path
1024	  default-*terminates* user procs, so a real `SIGCHLD` to the handler-less
1025	  parent would kill it; async `SIGCHLD` waits for Phase 5 handlers. No new priv
1026	  wiring (PM↔VM, PM↔SCHED, child↔`USER_PRIV_ID` edges all pre-exist). Stub E
1027	  rewritten from the 4.5 `PM_GETPID` loop into a fork/exit/wait loop: fork, and
1028	  on the reply `m_type` branch child→`PM_EXIT(0)` vs parent→`PM_WAIT`→loop.
1029	  Verified in QEMU over 25 s: eleven `[as]` lines; `[ksys SYS_PRIVCTL] target=E`
1030	  releasing E then each child; a sustained fork loop (69 cycles, still forking at
1031	  t≈25 s) all reusing child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint
1032	  generation (`0x10 → 0x220010` — proof of `okendpt` slot/ASID recycling and
1033	  full reap between cycles), `[ksys SYS_FORK]`/`SYS_EXIT] target=E nr=16 freed=2`

1035	  SIGSEGV, six RS `[alarm]` fires, SCHED `[noq]` delegation all intact; every
1036	  traced `result=0` (bar D's designed kill), zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
1037	  Host: `cargo test --workspace` green (new `mproc` fork/wait, `region::fork`,
1038	  `callnr` contiguity tests); `cargo check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` +
1039	  fmt clean.
1040	- **Slice 4.7** ◀ next — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
1041	  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
1042	  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
1043	  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
1044	  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
1045	  target.
1046	- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**
1047	  init (a freestanding Rust ELF loaded into `INIT_PROC_NR=10` by the archive)
1048	  forks children that exec the worker, `wait`s, and respawns in a loop. Retire or
1049	  demote the A/B/C/D stubs now that init + real procs are the live exercise. Real
1050	  mdBook *Servers* chapter (`book/src/servers/overview.md`); CLAUDE.md Phase-4
1051	  conventions; flip every slice marker and write the Phase-4-complete aggregate
1052	  paragraph.
1053	
1054	Aggregate scope (Phase 4 as a whole):
1055	
1056	- `minix-ipc`: NOTIFY + SENDNB primitives
1057	- `server-rt`: SEF startup + receive loop + init/signal callbacks (minimal subset)
1058	- Multi-module boot-image archive (MXBI) + generalized server loader
1059	- Kernel calls made real: `SYS_FORK`, `SYS_EXEC`, `SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`,
1060	  `SYS_SCHEDULE`, `SYS_SETALARM`, plus new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` and a minimal signal
1061	  mechanism; delegatable scheduler (`Proc::scheduler`)
1062	- Servers (all static-allocation, no heap): DS (endpoint registry), SCHED (real
1063	  user-space policy), RS (heartbeat monitor), VFS (skeletal boot), PM (process
1064	  table, fork, exec, exit, wait, getpid, minimal signals)
1065	- init (PID 1) forking/exec/waiting; embedded "worker" binary as the exec target
1066	- **Milestone:** Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts
1067	
1068	### Phase 5: musl Fork + File Systems
1069	
1070	- Add `src/minix/` to musl fork with IPC wrappers
1071	- Generate C headers from `kernel-shared` via cbindgen
1072	- `fs/mfs/`: MINIX File System server (Rust, MinixFS v3 on-disk format)
1073	- `fs/pfs/`: Pipe File System
1074	- `drivers/memory/`: /dev/null, /dev/zero, ramdisk
1075	- initramfs for early boot before disk driver
1076	- **Milestone:** C "Hello World" compiled against musl runs on minix.rs
1077	
1078	### Phase 6: VirtIO Drivers
1079	
1080	- `drivers/driver-rt/`: VirtIO MMIO transport (aarch64), virtqueue management, BDEV/CDEV protocol
1081	- `drivers/virtio-blk/`: Block device
1082	- `drivers/virtio-console/`: TTY
1083	- `drivers/virtio-net/`: Network (packet I/O only; TCP/IP stack is later)
1084	- Root filesystem on VirtIO disk
1085	- **Milestone:** Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root
1086	
1087	### Phase 7: Userland
1088	
1089	- `userland/init/`: Run /etc/rc, respawn gettys
1090	- `userland/sh/`: Simple shell (pipes, redirects, background jobs, builtins)
1091	- `userland/coreutils/`: Multi-call binary -- ls, cat, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, rmdir, echo, wc, head, tail, grep, chmod, pwd, env, sleep, date, uname, kill, ps, true, false, test
1092	- **Milestone:** Login, navigate filesystem, run scripts
1093	
1094	### Phase 8: x86_64 Port
1095	
1096	- `kernel/src/arch/x86_64/`: GDT, IDT, APIC, SYSCALL/SYSRET, 4-level page tables, context switch
1097	- `musl/src/minix/_ipc_x86_64.S`
1098	- VirtIO PCI transport
1099	- `tools/qemu-run-x86_64.sh`
1100	- **Milestone:** Same minix.rs boots on `qemu-system-x86_64`
1101	
1102	### Phase 9: Documentation + Polish
1103	
1104	- Comprehensive rustdoc across all crates
1105	- Architecture guide for students
1106	- End-to-end syscall trace walkthrough ("How read() works")
1107	- IPC latency + context switch benchmarks
1108	- Test suite (kernel unit tests, IPC stress, POSIX conformance)
1109	- **Milestone:** Teaching-ready OS with documentation
1110	
1111	---
1112	
1113	## Key Design Decisions
1114	
1115	**Rust kernel, not incremental port:** MINIX 3's C kernel uses raw pointers, global mutable state, and macro-heavy abstractions that resist incremental Rustification. A greenfield kernel uses proper Rust patterns from the start (enums for flags, Result for errors, indices not pointers for linked lists).
1116	
1117	**`unsafe` boundaries in kernel:** (a) Process table access (`UnsafeCell` static array), (b) user-space memory copies, (c) hardware register access (inline asm), (d) assembly entry/exit paths. Each `unsafe` block gets a `// SAFETY:` comment.
1118	
1119	**musl over Rust libc:** musl is well-tested, BSD-compatible, and complete. The MINIX-specific work is confined to ~100 syscall wrappers + IPC assembly stub. The rest of musl (stdio, string, math, locale) works unchanged.
1120	
1121	**Servers in Rust:** Shared `kernel-shared` message types provide compile-time IPC protocol verification. C servers (linked against musl) also work since the IPC mechanism is language-agnostic.
1122	
1123	**Multi-call coreutils:** Single `coreutils` binary dispatching on argv[0] (like BusyBox) minimizes disk space and simplifies building.
1124	
1125	---
1126	
1127	## Verification Strategy
1128	
1129	Each phase has a concrete milestone testable in QEMU (aarch64 primary):
1130	
1131	- Phase 0: `docs/` complete, directory tree navigable, CLAUDE.md in place
1132	- Phase 1: `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt` -> serial output visible
1133	- Phase 2: Ping-pong test via IPC verified by serial trace
1134	- Phase 3: Boot processes run in separate address spaces
1135	- Phase 4: Server initialization sequence completes to init
1136	- Phase 5: `printf("Hello\n")` from C program reaches serial
1137	- Phase 6: Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root
1138	- Phase 7: Interactive shell session
1139	- Phase 8: Same tests pass on `qemu-system-x86_64`
1140	- Phase 9: `cargo doc`, test suite green
1141	
1142	QEMU flags for aarch64 dev:
1143	```
1144	qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -m 256M \
1145	  -serial stdio -no-reboot -d int,cpu_reset -D qemu.log \
1146	  -drive file=minixrs.img,format=raw,if=virtio \
1147	  -device virtio-net-device
1148	```
1149	GDB: add `-s -S` then `rust-gdb -ex "target remote :1234"`
1150	
1151	---
1152	
1153	## Critical Reference Files (MINIX 3)
1154	
1155	Paths are relative to the MINIX 3 source tree root (under the `minix/` subdirectory
1156	for MINIX-specific code). See https://github.com/Stichting-MINIX-Research-Foundation/minix
1157	
1158	| Purpose | Path in MINIX 3 tree |
1159	|---------|---------------------|
1160	| IPC implementation (translate to Rust) | `kernel/proc.c` |
1161	| Process/privilege structures | `kernel/proc.h`, `kernel/priv.h` |
1162	| Kernel call dispatch | `kernel/system.c` |
1163	| x86_64 entry points | `kernel/arch/x86_64/mpx.S` |
1164	| Message definitions | `include/minix/ipc.h` |
1165	| IPC constants | `include/minix/ipcconst.h` |
1166	| Server endpoints + call numbers | `include/minix/com.h`, `include/minix/callnr.h` |
1167	| User-space IPC stubs | `lib/libc/arch/x86_64/sys/_ipc.S` |
1168	| _syscall() wrapper | `lib/libc/sys/syscall.c` |
1169	| POSIX wrappers (template for musl) | `lib/libc/sys/*.c` |
1170	| SEF framework | `lib/libsys/sef.c` |
1171	| Boot process table | `kernel/table.c` |
1172	| Limine integration design | `BOOT.md` (at repo root) |

frames. Ten new host tests cover both. The VM server (`main.rs`) dispatches
  `VM_MMAP → handle_mmap` (reply chosen base in payload `0..8`) and
  `VM_MUNMAP → handle_munmap`, which loops `SYS_VMCTL(VMCTL_PT_UNMAP)` over the
  returned range, ignoring the harmless `EINVAL` a never-faulted page returns.
  No kernel dispatch, `do_vmctl`, or priv-wiring changes — mmap/munmap ride the
  same D→VM SENDREC edge `install_stub_d_priv` already opened for brk.
  `user_stub.S` extends stub D after its brk sequence: `VM_MMAP(0x2000)` →
  stash the returned base in callee-saved `x22` → touch it (faults once, VM
  maps it) → `VM_MUNMAP(x22, 0x2000)` → steady loop over the two heap pages only
  (the mmap page is now unmapped). Docs: a real mdBook *Memory Management*
  chapter (`book/src/memory/overview.md`) written from source — frame
  allocator, per-proc `AddrSpace`/TTBR0/ASID, the page-fault → VM flow,
  `SYS_VMCTL`, and brk/mmap/munmap — plus CLAUDE.md notes on VM region kinds and
  the `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP`-on-hole behavior. Verified in QEMU over 8 s: five `[as]`
  lines (A/B/C/D + vm); three `[pf] proc=D` faults (`far=0x1000000`,
  `0x1004000`, `0x2000000`); three `[ksys VMCTL_PT_MAP] proc=D` (the two heap
  pages + `va=0x2000000`) and exactly one `[ksys VMCTL_PT_UNMAP] proc=D
  va=0x2000000` (the second, never-touched mmap page is a silent kernel
  `EINVAL`); D then round-robins with no re-fault; A↔B ping-pong + C
  `SYS_GETINFO` intact; zero nonzero results, no panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.

Aggregate scope (Phase 3 as a whole):

- `kernel/src/mm/`: physical frame allocator
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs`: per-process page-table API
- Per-proc TTBR0 + ASID allocator; context switch updates TTBR0_EL1
- EL1 page-fault handler routes to VM via kernel-originated SEND
- `kernel/src/system/do_vmctl.rs`: real SYS_VMCTL subcalls
- `kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`: minimal ELF loader for VM bootstrap
- `servers/vm/`: receive loop, region tracking, page-fault resolution,
  brk, mmap (all static-allocation; no heap allocator in VM)
- **Milestone:** Boot processes each have isolated address spaces; VM
  handles page faults

### Phase 4: Core Servers (PM, VFS, RS, DS, SCHED) + init

Phase 4 is split into 8 PR-sized slices (decomposition tracked in
`~/.claude/plans/go-ahead-with-phase-dapper-rain.md`). Each slice independently
builds, boots, and prints observable progress — same cadence as Phases 2–3. The
Phase 4 milestone ("Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts")
is satisfied at the end of slice 4.8.

Two scope decisions shape the phase: **exec is real but boot-embedded** (no
filesystem/musl until Phase 5, so `SYS_EXEC` loads ELF binaries packed into the
boot-image archive — the same archive that loads the servers — and Phase 5 later
swaps the source to a VFS file with no PM/kernel rework); and **scheduling moves
to a real user-space SCHED** by making the kernel scheduler *delegatable* rather
than replacing it (a per-proc `scheduler` endpoint defaults to kernel-scheduled,
and on quantum exhaustion the kernel either requeues or notifies the proc's
user-space scheduler). The boot `IMAGE` in `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` already
lists pm/vfs/rs/ds/sched/init with correct priv flags, and `init_boot_image`
already fills their `ipc_to` / `k_call_mask`, so loading a server needs only an
ELF + the generalized `load_boot_server` path — no new boot priv wiring.

- **Slice 4.1** ✓ shipped (PR #23, merged 2026-06-14) —
  `server-rt` SEF framework + migrate VM onto it + finish
  `minix-ipc`. Add `ipc_notify` / `ipc_sendnb` (new SVC `primitive` values).
  Build `server-rt`: `sef_startup()` (learn own endpoint/name via
  `SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI)`, run `init_fresh` callback) and `sef_receive()`
  (wrap `ipc_receive(ANY, …)`, intercept SEF control messages — ping/signal/init
  — and return only application messages), with static function-pointer callback
  registration (no heap; minimal subset vs MINIX `lib/libsys/sef.c`). Port
  `servers/vm` to the SEF loop (handlers unchanged) so VM is live regression
  coverage for the framework before any new server depends on it.
- **Slice 4.2** ✓ shipped (PR #24, merged 2026-06-21) — Multi-module boot image
  + DS server + VFS skeletal boot.

  New PM requests `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) let a user
  proc drive the lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape: user → PM, never
  user → kernel). `handle_fork` owns the tree — allocate a child `mproc` slot
  (fork pool `[16, NR_MPROCS)`, slot index = child kernel proc-nr), `SYS_FORK`
  (kernel clones the *frozen* child), `VM_FORK` (new `0xC04` request; VM's
  `region::fork` clones the parent's `ClientRegions`, `MAX_CLIENTS` widened
  16→32 to address the fork pool), `SCHEDULING_START` (SCHED schedules the
  still-frozen child — verified safe: `rts_unset` only enqueues on the
  last-block-bit clear, so `SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_PRIVCTL` leave it a blocked

  freeze, then a reply to **both** halves of the shared SENDREC (child gets
  `m_type = 0`, parent gets the child pid — MINIX fork-returns-twice). `mproc`

  free-slot allocator / zombie-and-reap helpers (all pure `*_in`, host-tested).
  `handle_exit` = `SCHEDULING_STOP` (before teardown, while the endpoint is
  valid) + `SYS_EXIT` + zombie; `handle_wait` reaps a zombie or suspends the
  parent (no reply) until `handle_exit` wakes it. **Scope decision:** parent
  notification is the zombie + wait-reap handshake only — the kernel signal path
  default-*terminates* user procs, so a real `SIGCHLD` to the handler-less
  parent would kill it; async `SIGCHLD` waits for Phase 5 handlers. No new priv
  wiring (PM↔VM, PM↔SCHED, child↔`USER_PRIV_ID` edges all pre-exist). Stub E

Aggregate scope (Phase 4 as a whole):

- `minix-ipc`: NOTIFY + SENDNB primitives
- `server-rt`: SEF startup + receive loop + init/signal callbacks (minimal subset)
- Multi-module boot-image archive (MXBI) + generalized server loader
- Kernel calls made real: `SYS_FORK`, `SYS_EXEC`, `SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`,
  `SYS_SCHEDULE`, `SYS_SETALARM`, plus new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` and a minimal signal
  mechanism; delegatable scheduler (`Proc::scheduler`)
- Servers (all static-allocation, no heap): DS (endpoint registry), SCHED (real
  user-space policy), RS (heartbeat monitor), VFS (skeletal boot), PM (process
  table, fork, exec, exit, wait, getpid, minimal signals)
- init (PID 1) forking/exec/waiting; embedded "worker" binary as the exec target
- **Milestone:** Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts

### Phase 5: musl Fork + File Systems

- Add `src/minix/` to musl fork with IPC wrappers
- Generate C headers from `kernel-shared` via cbindgen
- `fs/mfs/`: MINIX File System server (Rust, MinixFS v3 on-disk format)
- `fs/pfs/`: Pipe File System
- `drivers/memory/`: /dev/null, /dev/zero, ramdisk
- initramfs for early boot before disk driver
- **Milestone:** C "Hello World" compiled against musl runs on minix.rs

### Phase 6: VirtIO Drivers

- `drivers/driver-rt/`: VirtIO MMIO transport (aarch64), virtqueue management, BDEV/CDEV protocol
- `drivers/virtio-blk/`: Block device
- `drivers/virtio-console/`: TTY
- `drivers/virtio-net/`: Network (packet I/O only; TCP/IP stack is later)
- Root filesystem on VirtIO disk
- **Milestone:** Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root

### Phase 7: Userland

- `userland/init/`: Run /etc/rc, respawn gettys
- `userland/sh/`: Simple shell (pipes, redirects, background jobs, builtins)
- `userland/coreutils/`: Multi-call binary -- ls, cat, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, rmdir, echo, wc, head, tail, grep, chmod, pwd, env, sleep, date, uname, kill, ps, true, false, test
- **Milestone:** Login, navigate filesystem, run scripts

### Phase 8: x86_64 Port

- `kernel/src/arch/x86_64/`: GDT, IDT, APIC, SYSCALL/SYSRET, 4-level page tables, context switch
- `musl/src/minix/_ipc_x86_64.S`
- VirtIO PCI transport
- `tools/qemu-run-x86_64.sh`
- **Milestone:** Same minix.rs boots on `qemu-system-x86_64`

### Phase 9: Documentation + Polish

- Comprehensive rustdoc across all crates
- Architecture guide for students
- End-to-end syscall trace walkthrough ("How read() works")
- IPC latency + context switch benchmarks
- Test suite (kernel unit tests, IPC stress, POSIX conformance)
- **Milestone:** Teaching-ready OS with documentation

---

## Key Design Decisions

**Rust kernel, not incremental port:** MINIX 3's C kernel uses raw pointers, global mutable state, and macro-heavy abstractions that resist incremental Rustification. A greenfield kernel uses proper Rust patterns from the start (enums for flags, Result for errors, indices not pointers for linked lists).

**`unsafe` boundaries in kernel:** (a) Process table access (`UnsafeCell` static array), (b) user-space memory copies, (c) hardware register access (inline asm), (d) assembly entry/exit paths. Each `unsafe` block gets a `// SAFETY:` comment.

**musl over Rust libc:** musl is well-tested, BSD-compatible, and complete. The MINIX-specific work is confined to ~100 syscall wrappers + IPC assembly stub. The rest of musl (stdio, string, math, locale) works unchanged.

**Servers in Rust:** Shared `kernel-shared` message types provide compile-time IPC protocol verification. C servers (linked against musl) also work since the IPC mechanism is language-agnostic.

**Multi-call coreutils:** Single `coreutils` binary dispatching on argv[0] (like BusyBox) minimizes disk space and simplifies building.

---

## Verification Strategy

Each phase has a concrete milestone testable in QEMU (aarch64 primary):

- Phase 0: `docs/` complete, directory tree navigable, CLAUDE.md in place
- Phase 1: `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt` -> serial output visible
- Phase 2: Ping-pong test via IPC verified by serial trace
- Phase 3: Boot processes run in separate address spaces
- Phase 4: Server initialization sequence completes to init
- Phase 5: `printf("Hello\n")` from C program reaches serial
- Phase 6: Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root
- Phase 7: Interactive shell session
- Phase 8: Same tests pass on `qemu-system-x86_64`
- Phase 9: `cargo doc`, test suite green

QEMU flags for aarch64 dev:
```
qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -m 256M \
  -serial stdio -no-reboot -d int,cpu_reset -D qemu.log \
  -drive file=minixrs.img,format=raw,if=virtio \
  -device virtio-net-device
```
GDB: add `-s -S` then `rust-gdb -ex "target remote :1234"`

---

## Critical Reference Files (MINIX 3)

Paths are relative to the MINIX 3 source tree root (under the `minix/` subdirectory
for MINIX-specific code). See https://github.com/Stichting-MINIX-Research-Foundation/minix

| Purpose | Path in MINIX 3 tree |
|---------|---------------------|
| IPC implementation (translate to Rust) | `kernel/proc.c` |
| Process/privilege structures | `kernel/proc.h`, `kernel/priv.h` |
| Kernel call dispatch | `kernel/system.c` |
| x86_64 entry points | `kernel/arch/x86_64/mpx.S` |
| Message definitions | `include/minix/ipc.h` |
| IPC constants | `include/minix/ipcconst.h` |
| Server endpoints + call numbers | `include/minix/com.h`, `include/minix/callnr.h` |
| User-space IPC stubs | `lib/libc/arch/x86_64/sys/_ipc.S` |
| _syscall() wrapper | `lib/libc/sys/syscall.c` |
| POSIX wrappers (template for musl) | `lib/libc/sys/*.c` |
| SEF framework | `lib/libsys/sef.c` |
| Boot process table | `kernel/table.c` |
| Limine integration design | `BOOT.md` (at repo root) |

input.description: Explore kernel SYS_FORK/exec path
input.prompt:
I'm planning Slice 4.7 of the minix.rs kernel project (a Rust reimplementation of MINIX 3). This slice implements `SYS_EXEC`: the kernel builds a fresh AddrSpace, loads a boot-embedded ELF binary into it (resolved by name from the MXBI archive), sets up a minimal initial user stack, swaps the proc's ttbr0_pa, destroys the old address space, and sets entry point + SP.

The closest existing analog is `SYS_FORK` (added in slice 4.6a). I need to understand these existing pieces in the repo at /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs so I can design SYS_EXEC by reusing them.

Please explore and report back (medium thoroughness) with specific file paths, line numbers, and code excerpts for:

1. **SYS_FORK implementation**: Read `kernel/src/system/do_fork.rs` fully. Explain: how it allocates a free proc slot, copies the register frame, bumps the generation, allocates an ASID, does the eager address-space copy (walking the parent's TTBR0 and copying pages via HHDM), and how it zeroes sig_pending. Show the exact function signatures and how it's dispatched.

2. **SYS_EXIT / teardown**: Read `kernel/src/system/do_exit.rs`. I specifically need the address-space teardown sequence (walk_leaves, flush_tlb_asid, AddrSpace::destroy, asid::free_asid). Show how the old AS is destroyed — exec needs to destroy the old AS after swapping to the new one.

3. **Kernel-call dispatch**: Read `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`. Show how SYS_EXEC is currently stubbed (it should be an ENOSYS stub), the `kernel_call_dispatch` structure, how target-taking calls (SYS_VMCTL, SYS_SCHEDULE, SYS_FORK, SYS_EXIT) are routed vs caller-local calls (dispatch_caller_local), and the `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` const-assert that locks arm coverage. Show the current value of NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4.

4. **ELF loader**: Read `kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs` fully. I need to know: what functions exist for loading an ELF (the slice mentions `elf::load_into` — does it exist yet, or only a load function that allocates+maps its own frames?), the signatures, whether there's a way to load into an existing/given AddrSpace, and how PT_LOAD segments + BSS are handled. Report the entry point extraction and any return type.

5. **Boot image module resolution**: Read `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`. Show `module_by_name`, `module_by_proc_nr`, `iter()`, and the BootImage view API. Note that module_by_name may be `#[allow(dead_code)]` awaiting exec.

6. **AddrSpace API**: Read `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs`. Show AddrSpace::new, map_page / map_page_in, unmap_page_in, walk_pt / walk_leaves, destroy, and the Prot enum (RO_CODE, RW_DATA, etc.).

7. **ASID allocator**: Read `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/asid.rs`. Show alloc_asid, free_asid, the free-list recycling.

8. **callnr for SYS_EXEC**: Search `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` for SYS_EXEC and its value, plus the PM_RQ_BASE requests (PM_GETPID, PM_FORK, PM_EXIT, PM_WAIT) and their numbers. Report whether there's already a PM_EXEC request or if one needs adding.

Report concrete signatures and line references so I can plan reuse precisely. Do not propose a design — just report what exists.

input.description: Explore PM server exec integration
input.prompt:
I'm planning Slice 4.7 of the minix.rs project (Rust reimplementation of MINIX 3). This slice adds exec support: PM gains an `execve(name)` that selects a boot-embedded binary, and PM issues a kernel `SYS_EXEC` call to replace a process's address space. I need to understand the PM server thoroughly so I can add exec handling that matches existing patterns.

Repo is at /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs. Explore (medium-to-thorough) and report back with specific file paths, line numbers, and code excerpts:

1. **PM main loop & handlers**: Read `servers/pm/src/main.rs` fully. Show the SEF loop, how it dispatches on `msg.m_type`, and the existing handlers: handle_fork, handle_exit, handle_wait, PM_GETPID handling. I especially need to see how PM issues kernel calls — the exact pattern for calling `SYS_FORK`, `VM_FORK`, `SCHEDULING_START`, `SYS_PRIVCTL` from PM (message construction, endpoint targeting, SENDREC/SEND helpers). Show how PM replies to a SENDREC caller (both halves in fork).

2. **mproc table**: Read `servers/pm/src/mproc.rs` fully. Show the mproc slot struct (endpoint field, exit_status, MF_WAITING, pid/ppid), the fork pool allocator ([16, NR_MPROCS)), the endpoint field's generation-awareness, zombie/reap helpers, and the pure `*_in` host-tested helpers. I need to understand how a proc is looked up by endpoint/pid and how exec would find the caller's mproc slot.

3. **How PM constructs/sends kernel-call messages**: Find the helper(s) PM uses to do a SENDREC to SYSTEM (kernel calls) and to VM/SCHED. Look in `servers/pm/src/` and any shared helper in `server-rt` or `minix-ipc`. Show exact code for e.g. how handle_fork issues `SYS_FORK` (message layout: m_type, which payload fields carry parent endpoint / child proc-nr, how the reply is read).

4. **Stub E (the live demo driver)**: Slice 4.6b rewrote stub E into a fork/exit/wait loop. Find where stub E's blob is defined (likely `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S`) and how it's wired in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`. Show the stub E assembly and how PM releases/drives it. For 4.7, stub E (or a new demo) will need to also drive exec — I need to understand the current shape.

5. **PM priv wiring**: How is PM's ipc_to / k_call_mask set up? Search `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` and any `init_boot_image` for PM's kernel-call permissions. Does PM's k_call_mask already include SYS_EXEC (it may be granted by the NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 blanket fill)? Report the relevant code.

6. **Coverage exclusions**: Look at `sonar-project.properties` for `sonar.coverage.exclusions` — I need to know the pattern for excluding server main.rs / IPC-trap modules and whether pm/main.rs is already covered.

Report concrete signatures, message-field layouts, and line references. Do not propose a design — just report what exists.

input.description: Explore freestanding worker ELF build
input.prompt:
I'm planning Slice 4.7 of the minix.rs project (Rust reimplementation of MINIX 3). This slice needs "a tiny freestanding 'worker' ELF packed into the [MXBI boot-image] archive [as] the exec target." I need to understand exactly how existing freestanding server ELFs are built, linked, and packed so I can add a new minimal worker binary.

Repo is at /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs. Explore (medium-to-thorough) and report back with specific file paths, line numbers, and code excerpts:

1. **build.rs server packing**: Read `kernel/build.rs` fully. Show the `servers` array (the (crate, dir, proc_nr) rows), the `build_server(name, dir, ...)` function, `pack_mxbi(...)`, the isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR mechanism, the CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS linker-script override, and the rerun-if-changed watch list. I need to know exactly what to add to pack a new "worker" binary — including whether the worker needs a proc_nr (it's an exec target, not a boot-loaded server, so it may pack without being loaded at boot).

2. **A minimal existing server crate**: Read the smallest/simplest server for structure — look at `servers/vfs/` (skeletal) or `servers/ds/`. Show its `Cargo.toml`, its `user.ld` (or shared linker script), `src/main.rs` (the `#![no_std]`/`#![no_main]`, `_start`, panic handler, ELF section attributes like `#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", unsafe(link_section = ".text._start"))]`), and how the SPDX header + freestanding boilerplate look.

3. **user.ld linker script**: Find and read the `user.ld` used by servers (base 0x10_0000, page-aligned PT_LOADs). Report its full contents. Is there one per server or one shared?

4. **minix-ipc usage from a server**: Read `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs` (and the aarch64 SVC stub). Show the public API a freestanding ELF uses to do IPC (ipc_sendrec, ipc_send, ipc_receive, ipc_notify) — the worker will need to do at least a minimal SVC (e.g. PM_GETPID or an exit) to prove it ran. Report exact function signatures.

4b. **How does a loaded ELF prove it ran?** Since EL0 servers can't print, exec success is verified via kernel traces. Look at how a loaded server's activity is observed (e.g. via `[ipc]` / `[ksys]` traces keyed on the proc's endpoint). The worker will need to make some observable IPC call. Report how existing stubs/servers become observable.

5. **Server stack / entry expectations**: How does the kernel set up sp_el0 and the entry PC for a loaded server (so `_start` needs no stack setup)? Look in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` at `load_boot_server` and how entry/SP are set. This matters because exec must set up a "minimal initial user stack" — I need to see the current convention servers rely on.

6. **Cargo.toml workspace membership + publish=false**: Show the workspace members list in the root `Cargo.toml` and how server crates are marked `publish = false`. A new worker crate must be added there.

Report concrete file contents, signatures, and line references. Do not propose a design — just report what exists.

# Slice 4.7 SYS_EXEC — Reuse Survey

All paths absolute. Key headline: **`elf::load_into(elf, &mut aspace)` already exists** and is the exact "load into a given AddrSpace" primitive the slice needs; **`module_by_name` already exists** (dead-code-gated awaiting exec); **`SYS_EXEC` is wired as an ENOSYS stub**; and **there is no `PM_EXEC` request yet** — one must be added.

---

## 1. SYS_FORK implementation
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/system/do_fork.rs`

**Signature** (lines 72-76), dispatched from `mod.rs:201` as a target-taking call:
```rust
pub(super) fn do_fork(
    proc_table: &mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS],
    caller_nr: ProcNr,
    msg: &mut Message,
) -> i32
```

Flow:
- **Free slot selection**: fork does *not* search for a free slot — PM picks `child_nr` and passes it in `msg` payload `4..8`. do_fork validates it is in `0..NR_PROCS` (lines 88-90), converts via `proc_index` (line 92), and checks `RTS_SLOT_FREE` is set (lines 93-95). Parent resolved via `super::resolve_target` (line 80).
- **Parent gate** (lines 100-112): must be `RTS_RECEIVING`, not `RTS_SENDING`, no `MF_DELIVERMSG`, and `ttbr0_pa != 0`.
- **Register frame copy** (line 128 snapshot `parent.regs`, line 142 `c.regs = parent_regs`): copied verbatim; both resume after the same `svc`.
- **Generation bump**: NOT done here. The child consumes the slot's already-bumped stored endpoint (`c.endpoint`, line 182); `SYS_EXIT`'s `free_slot` did the bump.
- **ASID allocation** (line 123): `let child_asid = unsafe { asid::alloc_asid() };`
- **Eager address-space copy** (lines 116-120 call `copy_addrspace`, defined 207-257): builds `AddrSpace::new()`, then `walk_leaves(parent_ttbr0, ...)`: per leaf `alloc_frame`, `copy_nonoverlapping` through `phys_to_hhdm(pa)` → `phys_to_hhdm(new_pa)` for `FRAME_SIZE`, `flush_icache_range` if `prot.executable`, then `child.map_page(va, new_pa, prot)`. On error unwinds (frees mapped leaves via `walk_leaves`, then `child.destroy()`). Returns `(child.ttbr0_pa, pages)`.
- **sig_pending zeroing**: line 157 `c.sig_pending = 0;` (part of the "fresh, must not leak" block, lines 153-181). Also zeroes user_time, sys_time, alarm_at, caller_q, q_link, send/deliver msgs, page_fault_state.
- Child left frozen: `c.rts_flags.store(RTS_RECEIVING | RTS_NO_PRIV, ...)` (lines 180-181), not enqueued.
- Writes child endpoint back to `msg` payload `0..4` (line 185).

**Payload layout**: in — parent endpoint `0..4` (i32), child proc nr `4..8` (i32); out — child endpoint `0..4` (i32).

---

## 2. SYS_EXIT / teardown
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/system/do_exit.rs`

**Signature** (lines 72-77), dispatched `mod.rs:200`:
```rust
pub(super) fn do_exit(
    proc_table: &mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS],
    priv_table: &mut [Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS],
    caller_nr: ProcNr,
    msg: &mut Message,
) -> i32
```

**Address-space teardown** is factored into `teardown_addrspace(ttbr0_pa: u64, dead_asid: u8) -> u64` (lines 209-231) — this is the exact reusable sequence exec needs for destroying the *old* AS after swap:
```rust
fn teardown_addrspace(ttbr0_pa: u64, dead_asid: u8) -> u64 {
    if ttbr0_pa == 0 { return 0; }
    let mut freed: u64 = 0;
    let _ = walk_leaves(ttbr0_pa, &mut |_va, pa, _prot| {
        free_frame(Frame::from_addr(pa));
        freed += 1;
        Ok(())
    });
    unsafe { mmu::flush_tlb_asid(dead_asid) };   // must precede destroy? see note
    AddrSpace { ttbr0_pa }.destroy();
    unsafe { asid::free_asid(dead_asid) };
    freed
}
```
Order (important for exec): **walk_leaves → free each leaf → flush_tlb_asid → AddrSpace{ttbr0_pa}.destroy() → asid::free_asid**. The leaf walk MUST precede `destroy` (destroy frees the tables the walk reads). `AddrSpace { ttbr0_pa }` is constructed by struct literal directly (the struct has a single public field). Note the safety comment (lines 222-224): the dead AS is never the active TTBR0 — for exec you must swap `ttbr0_pa`/ASID on the proc *before* tearing the old one down, so the same "not active" invariant holds.

`free_slot` (lines 237-257) also zeroes sig_pending and bumps generation — exec does NOT free the slot (the proc lives on), so this is not reused, but shows the field-reset pattern.

---

## 3. Kernel-call dispatch
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/system/mod.rs`

- **SYS_EXEC currently stubbed**: `dispatch_caller_local` line 224: `SYS_EXEC => stubs::do_exec(caller, caller_priv, msg),` — routed as a **caller-local** call, returning `ENOSYS`.
- **Dispatch structure**: `kernel_call_sendrec` (line 90) → `kernel_call_dispatch` (line 170). The latter range-checks `m_type - KERNEL_CALL` against `NR_SYS_CALLS`, gates on `k_call_mask` via `get_call_bit` (line 187), then a `match msg.m_type` routes **target-taking** calls first (lines 193-204): `SYS_VMCTL`, `SYS_SCHEDULE`, `SYS_SCHEDCTL`, `SYS_KILL`, `SYS_GETKSIG`, `SYS_ENDKSIG`, `SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_FORK`, `SYS_PRIVCTL` — these get the full `proc_table` (+ `priv_table` for the ones touching notify bitmaps) + `caller_nr`. Everything else falls through to `dispatch_caller_local(caller, caller_priv, msg)` (line 210) with a single re-borrowed caller slot.
- **`resolve_target`** helper (lines 64-74): `SELF` → caller index, else `okendpt`. Shared by every target-taking handler; exec would use this to resolve its target (or operate on the caller).
- **The const-assert** locking arm coverage is in `dispatch_caller_local` (lines 218-221):
```rust
const _: () = assert!(
    NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 18,
    "expand kernel_call_dispatch when a new SYS_* lands",
);
```
Since `SYS_EXEC` already has a number and a stub arm, no new number is added, so this `== 18` assert would remain satisfied — but see §8.

**Design note for exec**: exec must swap the proc's `ttbr0_pa`/`asid` and tear down the *old* AS, which acts on a proc's whole slot + touches the frame allocator; it is currently a caller-local arm. If exec targets the caller only (MINIX self-exec model), the caller-local slot suffices; if PM names a target, it would need promotion to the target-taking match block like SYS_FORK/SYS_EXIT.

---

## 4. ELF loader
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`

**`elf::load_into` ALREADY EXISTS** (lines 88-133) — this is exactly the "load into a given AddrSpace" primitive:
```rust
pub fn load_into(elf: &[u8], aspace: &mut AddrSpace) -> Result<u64, ElfError>
```
Returns the entry-point VA (`e_entry`, read at line 110, returned line 132). There is **no** separate "allocate+map its own frames" variant — `load_into` allocates frames internally and maps them into the passed-in `aspace`. Validates magic/class/data/`ET_EXEC`/`EM_AARCH64`/`e_phentsize == 56`, walks program headers, and calls `load_segment` for each `PT_LOAD`.

`load_segment` (lines 135-200): requires page-aligned vaddr+offset and `p_filesz <= p_memsz` (else `Misaligned`); rejects W^X (`WriteExec`); maps `p_memsz.div_ceil(PAGE_SIZE)` pages via `alloc_frame` (frames are zeroed on hand-out, so **BSS tail is satisfied for free**) + `aspace.map_page(va, frame.addr(), prot)`. Copies file bytes through `phys_to_hhdm(frame.addr())`, `flush_icache_range` for executable pages. Prot mapping: `(false,true)→RO_CODE`, `(true,false)→RW_DATA`, `(false,false)→RO_DATA`.

`ElfError` enum (lines 25-48): Truncated, BadMagic, BadClass, BadData, BadMachine, BadType, BadPhentsize, Misaligned, WriteExec, `Map(MapError)` (with `From<MapError>` impl). All are `#[allow(dead_code)]`-free — publicly usable.

**Caveat**: `load_into` maps segments but does NOT set up a stack — exec must map the initial user stack itself (see §9).

---

## 5. Boot image module resolution
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`

`BootImage` is a zero-copy view; `BootImage::get()` (lines 53-77) validates the MXBI header and every record's bounds.

- **`module_by_name`** (lines 113-120), `#[allow(dead_code)]` with comment "consumed by slice 4.7 (SYS_EXEC of a boot-embedded binary)":
```rust
pub fn module_by_name(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&'static [u8]>
```
- **`module_by_proc_nr`** (lines 102-109), `#[allow(dead_code)]` "used by later Phase-4 slices (RS restart, exec)":
```rust
pub fn module_by_proc_nr(&self, nr: ProcNr) -> Option<&'static [u8]>
```
- **`iter`** (lines 92-98): `pub fn iter(&self) -> BootImageIter` yielding `(ProcNr, &'static [u8])` in archive/load order.

Both accessors return a `&'static [u8]` ELF payload — directly feedable to `elf::load_into`. The module doc (lines 22-23) explicitly names `module_by_name` as "reused by exec in slice 4.7". Module gated on `target_os = "none"`.

---

## 6. AddrSpace API
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs` (whole file `#![allow(dead_code)]`)

- **Struct** (lines 87-90): `pub struct AddrSpace { pub ttbr0_pa: u64 }` — single public field, so `AddrSpace { ttbr0_pa }` literal construction works (as do_exit does).
- **`AddrSpace::new() -> Result<Self, MapError>`** (lines 95-101): allocates the L0 root frame only.
- **`map_page(&mut self, va, pa, prot) -> Result<(), MapError>`** (line 108) → delegates to free fn **`map_page_in(ttbr0_pa, va, pa, prot) -> Result<(), MapError>`** (line 172, operates on a live proc's tree given only `ttbr0_pa`).
- **`unmap_page(&mut self, va) -> Option<u64>`** (line 122) → free fn **`unmap_page_in(ttbr0_pa, va) -> Option<u64>`** (line 200).
- **`walk_pt(&self, va) -> Option<u64>`** (line 128).
- **`walk_leaves(ttbr0_pa, f: &mut dyn FnMut(u64,u64,Prot)->Result<(),MapError>) -> Result<(),MapError>`** (line 226): free function, visits every L3 leaf in ascending-VA order.
- **`destroy(self)`** (line 149): frees all L1/L2/L3 tables + L0 root; does NOT free leaf frames (caller's responsibility — hence walk_leaves-then-destroy in teardown).
- **`Prot`** (lines 37-58): `pub struct Prot { pub writable: bool, pub executable: bool }` with consts `RO_CODE` (r-x), `RW_DATA` (rw-), `RO_DATA` (r--).
- `MapError` (lines 72-83): OutOfMemory, Misaligned, OutOfRange, AlreadyMapped.

---

## 7. ASID allocator
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/asid.rs`

- **`pub unsafe fn alloc_asid() -> u8`** (lines 59-73): pops from the LIFO free list if non-empty, else mints `pool.next++`; panics at 255 live.
- **`pub unsafe fn free_asid(asid: u8)`** (lines 86-99): asserts `asid != 0`, asserts it was allocated (`< pool.next`), asserts no double-free (O(255) scan), pushes onto free list. **Contract (lines 77-79): caller must have already `mmu::flush_tlb_asid`'d the ASID's TLB entries before freeing.**
- Free-list recycling: `AsidPool { next: u16, free: [u8; 255], free_len: usize }` (lines 34-52), `UnsafeCell` + `unsafe impl Sync`. `FIRST_ASID = 1`, ASID 0 reserved.

For exec: allocate a fresh ASID for the new AS via `alloc_asid()`, install it on the proc, and free the *old* ASID (after `flush_tlb_asid`) via the do_exit teardown sequence.

---

## 8. callnr for SYS_EXEC
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`

- **`SYS_EXEC`** (line 17): `pub const SYS_EXEC: i32 = KERNEL_CALL + 3;` → **`0x603`**. Already defined and in the contiguity test (line 407) and `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` count.
- `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 = 18` (line 60). SYS_EXEC is already within this — no bump needed for the kernel-call number.
- **PM requests** (base `PM_RQ_BASE = 0x700`, line 160):
  - `PM_GETPID = PM_RQ_BASE` = **0x700** (line 167)
  - `PM_FORK = PM_RQ_BASE + 1` = **0x701** (line 176)
  - `PM_EXIT = PM_RQ_BASE + 2` = **0x702** (line 182)
  - `PM_WAIT = PM_RQ_BASE + 3` = **0x703** (line 190)
  - `NR_PM_MSGS = 4` (line 194)
- **There is NO `PM_EXEC` request.** To drive exec from userspace via PM, a new `PM_EXEC = PM_RQ_BASE + 4` (0x704) would need adding, `NR_PM_MSGS` bumped to 5, and the `pm_msgs_contiguous_from_base` test (lines 622-633) extended. The PM range has room: `const _: () = assert!(PM_RQ_BASE + (NR_PM_MSGS - 1) < VM_RQ_BASE)` (line 199, VM_RQ_BASE = 0xC00) still holds.

Stub location: `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/system/stubs.rs` line 25 `enosys_stub!(do_exec);` — the real handler replaces this, keeping the `do_exec` name (dispatch table untouched, per stubs.rs doc lines 8-10).

---

## 9. BONUS — closest analog for "load ELF + set entry/SP + swap AS"
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`

`load_boot_server(nr, elf, stack_va)` (lines 295-344) is the exact shape exec reuses at boot time:
1. `AddrSpace::new()` → `ttbr0_pa` (lines 299-300)
2. `let entry = elf::load_into(elf, &mut aspace)` (lines 302-303)
3. Map one zeroed RW stack page: `alloc_frame()` + `aspace.map_page(stack_va, frame.addr(), Prot::RW_DATA)` (lines 306-309)
4. `let asid = alloc_asid()` (line 311)
5. Set EL0 entry state on the proc (lines 316-320):
```rust
p.regs.elr_el1 = entry;
p.regs.sp_el0 = stack_va + PAGE_SIZE as u64;   // SP at top of the stack page
p.regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0;
p.ttbr0_pa = ttbr0_pa;
p.asid = asid;
```
6. `core::mem::forget(aspace)` (line 340) — the tree is now owned via `p.ttbr0_pa`; do NOT `destroy`.

Stack VAs are constants at the top of the file: `USER_STACK_VA_A..E` (lines 56-76, e.g. `0x0080_0000`), `SERVER_STACK_VA` used by servers. `build_stub` (line 359+) sets the same three regs fields (`elr_el1`/`sp_el0`/`spsr_el1`, lines 472-474). The register frame fields exec must set are therefore: `regs.elr_el1` (entry PC), `regs.sp_el0` (stack pointer), and, for a fresh stack, `regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0`. For exec the key difference from boot is that the proc already has a live `ttbr0_pa`/`asid` — exec swaps in the new pair, then tears down the old pair via the do_exit `teardown_addrspace` sequence (§2), and must `mem::forget` the new `AddrSpace` so the swapped-in tree is not destroyed.</result>

Since `SYS_EXEC` already has a number and a stub arm, no new number is added, so this `== 18` assert would remain satisfied — but see §8.

**Design note for exec**: exec must swap the proc's `ttbr0_pa`/`asid` and tear down the *old* AS, which acts on a proc's whole slot + touches the frame allocator; it is currently a caller-local arm. ## 4. ELF loader
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`

**`elf::load_into` ALREADY EXISTS** (lines 88-133) — this is exactly the "load into a given AddrSpace" primitive:
```rust
pub fn load_into(elf: &[u8], aspace: &mut AddrSpace) -> Result<u64, ElfError>
```
Returns the entry-point VA (`e_entry`, read at line 110, returned line 132). There is **no** separate "allocate+map its own frames" variant — `load_into` allocates frames internally and maps them into the passed-in `aspace`. Validates magic/class/data/`ET_EXEC`/`EM_AARCH64`/`e_phentsize == 56`, walks program headers, and calls `load_segment` for each `PT_LOAD`.

`load_segment` (lines 135-200): requires page-aligned vaddr+offset and `p_filesz <= p_memsz` (else `Misaligned`); rejects W^X (`WriteExec`); maps `p_memsz.div_ceil(PAGE_SIZE)` pages via `alloc_frame` (frames are zeroed on hand-out, so **BSS tail is satisfied for free**) + `aspace.map_page(va, frame.addr(), prot)`. Copies file bytes through `phys_to_hhdm(frame.addr())`, `flush_icache_range` for executable pages. Prot mapping: `(false,true)→RO_CODE`, `(true,false)→RW_DATA`, `(false,false)→RO_DATA`.

`ElfError` enum (lines 25-48): Truncated, BadMagic, BadClass, BadData, BadMachine, BadType, BadPhentsize, Misaligned, WriteExec, `Map(MapError)` (with `From<MapError>` impl). All are `#[allow(dead_code)]`-free — publicly usable.

**Caveat**: `load_into` maps segments but does NOT set up a stack — exec must map the initial user stack itself (see §9).

## 9. BONUS — closest analog for "load ELF + set entry/SP + swap AS"
`/Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`

`load_boot_server(nr, elf, stack_va)` (lines 295-344) is the exact shape exec reuses at boot time:
1. `AddrSpace::new()` → `ttbr0_pa` (lines 299-300)
2. `let entry = elf::load_into(elf, &mut aspace)` (lines 302-303)
3. Map one zeroed RW stack page: `alloc_frame()` + `aspace.map_page(stack_va, frame.addr(), Prot::RW_DATA)` (lines 306-309)
4. `let asid = alloc_asid()` (line 311)
5. Set EL0 entry state on the proc (lines 316-320):
```rust
p.regs.elr_el1 = entry;
p.regs.sp_el0 = stack_va + PAGE_SIZE as u64;   // SP at top of the stack page
p.regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0;
p.ttbr0_pa = ttbr0_pa;
p.asid = asid;
```
6. `core::mem::forget(aspace)` (line 340) — the tree is now owned via `p.ttbr0_pa`; do NOT `destroy`.

Stack VAs are constants at the top of the file: `USER_STACK_VA_A..E` (lines 56-76, e.g. `0x0080_0000`), `SERVER_STACK_VA` used by servers. `build_stub` (line 359+) sets the same three regs fields (`elr_el1`/`sp_el0`/`spsr_el1`, lines 472-474). The register frame fields exec must set are therefore: `regs.elr_el1` (entry PC), `regs.sp_el0` (stack pointer), and, for a fresh stack, `regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0`. For exec the key difference from boot is that the proc already has a live `ttbr0_pa`/`asid` — exec swaps in the new pair, then tears down the old pair via the do_exit `teardown_addrspace` sequence (§2), and must `mem::forget` the new `AddrSpace` so the swapped-in tree is not destroyed.</result>
