  zeroed on alloc). No changes needed. (`kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`)
- `BootImage::module_by_name(name) -> Option<&'static [u8]>` — currently
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` "consumed by slice 4.7". (`kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`)
- `do_exit::teardown_addrspace(ttbr0_pa, dead_asid) -> u64` — walk_leaves +
  free_frame + `flush_tlb_asid` + `AddrSpace::destroy` + `free_asid`. The exact
  old-AS teardown exec needs. (`kernel/src/system/do_exit.rs:209`)
- `do_fork::do_fork` — the target-taking handler template + its
  `RTS_RECEIVING`/`!RTS_SENDING`/`!MF_DELIVERMSG` parent gate. (`do_fork.rs`)
- `load_boot_server` — the "AddrSpace::new + load_into + map stack + alloc_asid
  + set entry/SP/SPSR + mem::forget" analog. (`userland.rs:295`)
- `ArchRegisterFrame::EMPTY`; `STUB_SPSR_EL0 = 0x340`; `SERVER_STACK_VA`.
- PM's `handle_fork` multi-step kernel-call driver + `sys_fork`/`reply`/`wr_i32`
  helpers. (`servers/pm/src/main.rs`)

`kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`:

  helpers. (`servers/pm/src/main.rs`)

  blanket SRV_T fill (`NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 = 18`, unchanged).

### 1. kernel-shared — new PM request + name constant

  `PM_RQ_BASE + (NR_PM_MSGS-1) < VM_RQ_BASE` assert still holds (`0x704 < 0xC00`).
- Add `pub const EXEC_NAME_LEN: usize = 16;` (the SYS_EXEC payload name field;
  MXBI record names are `< 20`, "worker" fits).
- Extend the `pm_msgs_contiguous_from_base` host test to cover `PM_EXEC`.

### 2. kernel — `SYS_EXEC` handler (new `do_exec.rs`)
- New `kernel/src/system/do_exec.rs`, target-taking signature mirroring do_fork:
  `pub(super) fn do_exec(proc_table: &mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS], caller_nr: ProcNr, msg: &mut Message) -> i32`.
  Steps:
  1. Resolve target from payload `0..4` via `super::resolve_target`. **Reject
     SELF and target == caller** (`EINVAL`) — tearing down the active TTBR0
     mid-call is the hazard (same stance as `do_exit`).
  2. Read name from payload `4..4+EXEC_NAME_LEN` (NUL-terminated →
     `core::str::from_utf8`; `EINVAL` on bad UTF-8).
  3. `BootImage::get().module_by_name(name)` → ELF bytes; `ENOENT` (or `EINVAL`
     if `ENOENT` absent — verify errno set) if not found.

     `RTS_SENDING` clear, `MF_DELIVERMSG` clear (in the live flow it is
     mid-`SENDREC` to PM), else `EINVAL`. This is the last point a failure can
     leave the target on its old image cleanly.
  5. Build the new AS via a new arch helper (see §3): returns
     `(new_ttbr0, new_asid, entry, sp_top)`. On error → `ENOMEM`, target
     untouched.
  6. Capture the target's old `(ttbr0_pa, asid)`. Write the new AS + reset the
     frame onto the target slot: `regs = ArchRegisterFrame::EMPTY`;
     `regs.elr_el1 = entry`; `regs.sp_el0 = sp_top`;
     `regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0`; `ttbr0_pa = new_ttbr0`; `asid = new_asid`.

     `misc_flags &= !MF_DELIVERMSG`, `caller_q = None`, `deliver_msg` zeroed.
  7. `teardown_addrspace(old_ttbr0, old_asid)` (make it `pub(super)` in
     do_exit.rs; do_exec is a sibling under `system`). Safe: target ≠ caller,
     so the old AS is never the active TTBR0.
  8. Unblock the target: `sched::rts_unset(p, RTS_RECEIVING)` (capture-then-
     borrow-end pattern) — it enqueues when the last block bit clears. The proc
     keeps its `scheduler` (SCHED, from fork's `SCHEDULING_START`).
  9. Trace `[ksys SYS_EXEC] target=… name=… entry=… old_asid=… new_asid=…` with
     the head+modulo carve-out (mirror `FORK_TRACE_HEAD`/`EVERY`). Return `OK`.
- **No reply is delivered to the target** — the kernel resumes it at the new
  entry, no return value. (PM sends no reply on success either; see §5.)
- `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`: move `SYS_EXEC` from the caller-local match
  (delete the `SYS_EXEC => stubs::do_exec(...)` arm) into the target-taking
  `match` beside `SYS_FORK`: `SYS_EXEC => return do_exec::do_exec(proc_table, caller_nr, msg)`.
  Add `mod do_exec;`. The `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 18` const-assert is unchanged
  (no new call number; every call still has exactly one arm).
- `kernel/src/system/stubs.rs`: delete `enosys_stub!(do_exec);`.
- `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`: drop `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `module_by_name`
  (now consumed). Keep it on `module_by_proc_nr` (still for later RS restart).

### 3. kernel — arch helper to build a fresh user AS
`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`:
- Add `pub(crate) fn load_exec_image(elf: &[u8]) -> Result<ExecImage, MapError>`
  factoring the `load_boot_server` body: `AddrSpace::new()` → `elf::load_into`
  → map one zeroed RW stack page at `SERVER_STACK_VA` → `alloc_asid()` →
  `mem::forget(aspace)`; returns `{ ttbr0_pa, asid, entry, sp_top: SERVER_STACK_VA + PAGE_SIZE }`.
  Refactor `load_boot_server` to call it (keeps one source of truth). Reuses the
  server stack VA — safe because each proc has its own TTBR0 and the target's
  new AS is fresh.
- Expose `STUB_SPSR_EL0` as `pub(crate)` (do_exec sets `spsr_el1`).

### 4. Worker ELF (new crate `userland/worker`)
- `userland/worker/Cargo.toml` — `publish = false`, `[[bin]] minixrs-worker`,
  deps `minixrs-kernel-shared` + `minixrs-ipc` only (no server-rt/SEF — it's a
  plain user program). Add `userland/worker` to root `Cargo.toml` members.
- `userland/worker/user.ld` — copy of `servers/vfs/user.ld` (base `0x0010_0000`).
- `userland/worker/src/main.rs` — SPDX header; `#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)]`
  / `no_main`; `_start` → `main`; `#[panic_handler]` spin loop (same skeleton as
  `servers/vfs/src/main.rs` minus SEF). `main` does a few observable
  `ipc_sendrec(boot_endpoint(PM_PROC_NR), PM_GETPID)` calls (surfaces as `[ipc]`
  `caller=16 target=0x0` — proof the new image runs, with the child's preserved
  pid), then `ipc_sendrec(PM, PM_EXIT)` with status 0 → PM tears it down.

### 5. PM — `PM_EXEC` handler
`servers/pm/src/main.rs`:
- Dispatch arm: `PM_EXEC => handle_exec(system, &mut msg)`.
- `const EXEC_TARGET: &str = "worker";` (Phase 5 will thread a user-supplied
  name; the kernel path is already name-driven).
- `fn handle_exec(system, msg)`: `caller_e = msg.m_source`; `rc = sys_exec(system, caller_e, EXEC_TARGET)`;
  on `rc != OK` → `reply(caller_e, msg, rc)` (the proc is still blocked on the
  old image, gets the errno); on success → **no reply** (kernel resumed it).
- `fn sys_exec(system, target_e, name) -> i32`: `Message { m_type: SYS_EXEC }`;
  `wr_i32(0, target_e)`; copy `name.as_bytes()` into payload `4..4+len`
  (NUL-padded); `ipc_sendrec(system, &mut m)`; return `m.m_type`.

### 6. Demo — stub E child execs instead of exits
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S`: add `.equ PM_EXEC, 0x704`. Rewrite
  stub E's child branch (label `3:`) from `SENDREC PM_EXIT` to `SENDREC PM_EXEC`
  (no payload — PM hardcodes the name). After the `svc`, exec has replaced the
  image, so the following `b .` park is unreachable. Update the block comment
  (and the stale `userland.rs:224` "SENDREC PM_GETPID loop" comment left over
  from 4.5).

### 7. build.rs — pack the worker, skip it at boot
`kernel/build.rs`:
- Add a row to the `servers` array: `("minixrs-worker", workspace.join("userland/worker"), -1)`
  with a comment: `-1` = exec-only, packed for `module_by_name`, skipped by the
  boot loader.
- Watch `userland/worker/src` + its `user.ld`/`Cargo.toml` (rerun-if-changed).
`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` boot-load loop: skip non-loadable modules —
`for (nr, elf) in image.iter() { if nr.get() < 0 { continue; } load_boot_server(...); }`
(a negative proc_nr has no proc/priv slot; `load_boot_server`'s
`proc_slot_mut(nr).expect(...)` would otherwise panic). Optionally name the
sentinel `pub const EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR: i32 = -1;` in `kernel-shared/src/com.rs`.

### 8. Coverage + docs
- `sonar-project.properties`: add `userland/**/src/main.rs` to
  `sonar.coverage.exclusions` (freestanding entry point, no host-testable logic;
  future-proofs 4.8's init). PM's `main.rs` is already covered by
  `servers/**/src/main.rs`.
- `docs/plan.md`: flip **4.6b** → `✓ shipped (PR #29, merged 2026-07-18)`; flip
  **4.7** → `✓ ready (branch feature/phase-4-7-exec, pending merge)`; slide
  `◀ next` to **4.8**. Write the 4.7 summary paragraph in the established style.
- `CLAUDE.md`: add a slice-4.7 convention note (SYS_EXEC is target-taking + name
  in payload; `module_by_name` from the MXBI archive; exec-only worker packed
  with sentinel proc_nr `-1`, skipped by the boot loader; PM's `handle_exec`
  no-reply-on-success; exec preserves pid/priv/scheduler).

## Verification

1. **Host**: `cargo test --workspace` (new `PM_EXEC` contiguity test green);
   `cargo fmt --all --check`; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`.
2. **Kernel build**: `cargo kernel-aarch64` (the real compile gate; also builds
   + packs the worker into the MXBI archive).
3. **QEMU** (`timeout 25 cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release > /tmp/47.log 2>&1`,
   grep with `grep -a`). Expect:
   - `[as]` lines for the six servers + stubs A–E; **no `[as]` for the worker**
     (it is never boot-loaded).
   - Per fork cycle: `[ksys SYS_FORK] … nr=16 …` → `[ksys SYS_EXEC] target=… name=worker entry=… new_asid=…`
     → worker `[ipc]` `caller=16 … target=0x0` (its PM_GETPIDs) → `[ksys SYS_EXIT] target=… nr=16 freed=N`
     → parent's `PM_WAIT` reaps → next `SYS_FORK` reusing slot 16 with a
     monotonically advancing endpoint generation (proves exec + full teardown +
     reap + recycle).
   - Regressions intact: A↔B ping-pong, C `SYS_GETINFO`/`[noq]` delegation,
     D's `[pf]` sequence + the designed SIGSEGV, six RS `[alarm]` fires.
   - Zero `panic` / `el0_sync_unexpected`; every traced `result=0` (bar D's
     designed kill).

## Out of scope (deferred)
- User-supplied exec name/argv/envp marshaling (Phase 5 filesystem/musl).
- CoW / lazy image faulting (exec eagerly builds the full new AS).
- init (PID 1) forking+exec+wait loop and retiring the A–D stubs — **slice 4.8**.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Slice 4.7 — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary

## Context

Phase 4 builds the core MINIX servers. Slices 4.5/4.6 gave PM the process
lifecycle (`getpid`, `fork`, `exit`, `wait`) driven entirely through PM in the
POSIX shape (user → PM, never user → kernel). The one lifecycle piece still
missing is **exec** — replacing a process's program image in place. This slice
adds it: a user proc sends `PM_EXEC` to PM, PM issues the kernel call
`SYS_EXEC` naming that proc, and the kernel builds a fresh address space, loads
a boot-embedded ELF into it, resets the target to the new binary's entry point,
and tears down the old address space. The exec target is a tiny freestanding
"worker" ELF packed into the MXBI boot archive.

Exec is real but the binary source is **boot-embedded** (no filesystem/musl until
Phase 5): `SYS_EXEC` resolves the binary by name from the same MXBI archive that
loads the servers, via the already-present `BootImage::module_by_name`. Phase 5
later swaps the source to a VFS file with no PM/kernel rework. The live demo
turns the 4.6b fork/wait loop into **fork → child execs the worker → worker
exits → parent reaps → loop**, proving exec + slot recycling in one cycle.

Also reconcile a stale marker: **4.6b shipped as PR #29** (merged 2026-07-18, on
`main`) but `docs/plan.md` still labels it `◀ ready (pending merge)`.

## Key reuse (already in the tree)

     monotonically advancing endpoint generation (proves exec + full teardown +
     reap + recycle).
   - Regressions intact: A↔B ping-pong, C `SYS_GETINFO`/`[noq]` delegation,
     D's `[pf]` sequence + the designed SIGSEGV, six RS `[alarm]` fires.
   - Zero `panic` / `el0_sync_unexpected`; every traced `result=0` (bar D's
     designed kill).

## Out of scope (deferred)
- User-supplied exec name/argv/envp marshaling (Phase 5 filesystem/musl).
- CoW / lazy image faulting (exec eagerly builds the full new AS).
- init (PID 1) forking+exec+wait loop and retiring the A–D stubs — **slice 4.8**.

150	//
151	// Like the VM/DS/SEF/SCHED ranges these are *server IPC requests*, not kernel
152	// calls. SCHED's `0xF00` block is the last one below the IPC `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`
153	// marker (`0x1000`), so PM takes the free gap between the kernel-call range
154	// (`0x600..0x618`) and VM (`0xC00`). Numbering is minix.rs-specific — MINIX 3
155	// carries PM call numbers in `callnr.h`; those ABI numbers arrive with the
156	// musl wrappers in Phase 5.
157	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
158	
159	/// Base for PM server request `m_type` values.
160	pub const PM_RQ_BASE: i32 = 0x700;
161	
162	/// Client → PM: return the caller's process id. No request payload — the
163	/// kernel-stamped `m_source` names the caller. Reply: `m_type` *is* the pid
164	/// (MINIX convention: the result is the pid, >= 0; errors are negative, e.g.
165	/// `ESRCH` for a caller unknown to PM's mproc table), with the parent's pid
166	/// in payload `0..4` (i32) so `getppid` needs no second call.
167	pub const PM_GETPID: i32 = PM_RQ_BASE;
168	
169	/// User → PM: `fork()`. No request payload — the kernel-stamped `m_source` names
170	/// the parent. PM allocates a child slot, drives `SYS_FORK` + `VM_FORK` +
171	/// `SCHEDULING_START` + `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)`, then replies to **both**
172	/// halves of the shared blocked SENDREC: the child receives `m_type = 0`, the
173	/// parent receives `m_type = child_pid` (MINIX fork-returns-twice). On failure
174	/// the parent's reply carries a negative errno (`EAGAIN` if PM's process table
175	/// is full). (slice 4.6b)
176	pub const PM_FORK: i32 = PM_RQ_BASE + 1;
177	
178	/// User → PM: `exit(status)`. Exit status in payload `0..4` (i32). PM tears the
179	/// caller down (`SCHEDULING_STOP` + `SYS_EXIT`) and either wakes a `wait()`ing
180	/// parent or leaves a zombie for the parent's next `wait()`. The caller is dead
181	/// after `SYS_EXIT`, so PM sends **no** reply. (slice 4.6b)
182	pub const PM_EXIT: i32 = PM_RQ_BASE + 2;
183	
184	/// User → PM: `wait()` for any child. No request payload — `m_source` names the
185	/// parent. If a zombie child exists PM reaps it and replies immediately;
186	/// otherwise, with a live child, PM suspends the caller (no reply) until the
187	/// child exits. Reply: `m_type` *is* the reaped child's pid (>= 0), with the
188	/// encoded exit status in payload `0..4` (i32, `(status & 0xff) << 8`), or
189	/// `ECHILD` in `m_type` if the caller has no children. (slice 4.6b)
190	pub const PM_WAIT: i32 = PM_RQ_BASE + 3;
191	
192	/// Number of PM server requests defined so far. Locks the PM server's
193	/// dispatch coverage the way `NR_DS_REQUESTS` locks the DS server.
194	pub const NR_PM_MSGS: usize = 4;
195	
196	// The PM range sits strictly above the kernel-call range and strictly below
197	// VM's (and therefore every other) server request range and the NOTIFY marker.
198	const _: () = assert!(PM_RQ_BASE > KERNEL_CALL + (NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 as i32 - 1));
199	const _: () = assert!(PM_RQ_BASE + (NR_PM_MSGS as i32 - 1) < VM_RQ_BASE);
200	const _: () = assert!(PM_RQ_BASE + (NR_PM_MSGS as i32 - 1) < crate::ipc_const::NOTIFY_MESSAGE);
201	
202	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
203	// VM server request numbers — `m_type` values for messages addressed to VM.
204	//

280	/// populated its name, priority, endpoint, and privilege slot (`trap_mask =
281	/// SRV_T` → RECEIVE ANY + SEND to any active slot; full `k_call_mask`). So this
282	/// fills in only the address space + EL0 entry state. No `install_*_priv` helper
283	/// is needed.
284	///
285	/// `stack_va` is shared across servers ([`SERVER_STACK_VA`]); each gets its own
286	/// TTBR0, so the same VA maps a distinct frame per server.
287	///
288	/// The `AddrSpace` is `mem::forget`-ed for the same reason as [`build_stub`]:
289	/// the page-table tree is now owned via `Proc::ttbr0_pa`.
290	///
291	/// SAFETY: single-threaded boot; the only writer of `nr`'s proc slot and the
292	/// frame allocator here. Must run after `mm::init_from_limine_memmap` and
293	/// before `sched::run`. `nr` must be a boot server already populated by
294	/// `init_boot_image` (blocked on `RTS_NO_PRIV`).
295	unsafe fn load_boot_server(nr: ProcNr, elf: &[u8], stack_va: u64) {
296	    use crate::arch::aarch64::uart::Pl011;
297	    use core::fmt::Write;
298	
299	    let mut aspace = AddrSpace::new().expect("server AddrSpace::new failed");
300	    let ttbr0_pa = aspace.ttbr0_pa;
301	
302	    let entry =
303	        crate::boot_image::elf::load_into(elf, &mut aspace).expect("server ELF load failed");
304	
305	    // Stack: one zeroed RW page; SP starts at its top.
306	    let stack_frame = alloc_frame().expect("server stack frame alloc failed");
307	    aspace
308	        .map_page(stack_va, stack_frame.addr(), Prot::RW_DATA)
309	        .expect("map_page(server stack) failed");
310	
311	    let asid = unsafe { alloc_asid() };
312	
313	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; sole borrow of this server's slot.
314	    unsafe {
315	        let p = proc_slot_mut(nr).expect("server proc slot in range");
316	        p.regs.elr_el1 = entry;
317	        p.regs.sp_el0 = stack_va + PAGE_SIZE as u64;
318	        p.regs.spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0;
319	        p.ttbr0_pa = ttbr0_pa;
320	        p.asid = asid;
321	        p.next_ready = None;
322	        // Clear the boot RTS_NO_PRIV: the server now has an address space.
323	        p.rts_flags.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
324	
325	        let name = core::str::from_utf8(&p.name)
326	            .unwrap_or("?")
327	            .trim_end_matches('\0');
328	        let _ = writeln!(
329	            Pl011::new(),
330	            "[as] {} nr={} ttbr0_pa={:#x} asid={} entry={:#x}",
331	            name,
332	            p.nr.get(),
333	            p.ttbr0_pa,
334	            p.asid,
335	            entry,
336	        );
337	    }
338	
339	    // The page-table tree is durable via `ttbr0_pa`; don't run `destroy`.
340	    core::mem::forget(aspace);
341	
342	    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; no other PROC_TABLE / RUNQ borrow is live.
343	    unsafe { sched::enqueue(nr) };
344	}

374

content.content: Updated task #3 status

1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
2	// Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors
3	//! minix.rs VFS (virtual file system) server — skeletal boot (slice 4.2).
4	//!
5	//! This slice stands VFS up as a real boot server: it boots through the SEF
6	//! framework and publishes its endpoint to DS, proving the multi-server boot
7	//! path and the DS registry end to end. It does *no* file operations yet — the
8	//! PM↔VFS fork/exec work protocol needs file descriptors and is Phase 5 — so the
9	//! receive loop simply drops any application traffic that arrives.
10	//!
11	//! Built as a freestanding aarch64 ELF (see `servers/vfs/user.ld`), packed into
12	//! the kernel's boot-image archive by `kernel/build.rs`, and loaded into its own
13	//! per-process AddrSpace at boot by `arch::aarch64::userland::load_boot_server`.
14	
15	// Freestanding for the real (bare-metal) build, but a normal host binary under
16	// `cargo test` (no host tests yet — the SEF/IPC glue is QEMU-verified). The
17	// `_start` shim and panic handler are gated to `not(test)`.
18	#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)]
19	#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_main)]
20	
21	use minixrs_kernel_shared::Message;
22	use minixrs_kernel_shared::callnr::SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN;
23	use minixrs_kernel_shared::endpoint::Endpoint;
24	use minixrs_server_rt::{SefConfig, sef_publish_to_ds, sef_startup};
25	
26	/// ELF entry point. The kernel primes `SP_EL0` before `eret`, so `_start` can
27	/// dive straight into Rust without setting up a stack itself.
28	#[cfg(not(test))]
29	#[unsafe(no_mangle)]
30	#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", unsafe(link_section = ".text._start"))]
31	pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
32	    main()
33	}
34	
35	#[cfg_attr(test, allow(dead_code))]
36	fn main() -> ! {
37	    // `sef_startup` learns VFS's endpoint/name and runs `vfs_init`, which
38	    // publishes VFS's endpoint to DS. The publish SENDREC blocks until DS is in
39	    // its receive loop — safe at boot (DS's init does no IPC). No signal
40	    // handling. On startup failure there is no recovery and nothing to print.
41	    let sef = sef_startup(SefConfig {
42	        init_fresh: Some(vfs_init),
43	        signal_handler: None,
44	    })
45	    .unwrap_or_else(|_| {
46	        loop {
47	            core::hint::spin_loop()
48	        }
49	    });
50	
51	    let mut msg = Message {
52	        m_source: 0,
53	        m_type: 0,
54	        payload: [0u8; 96],
55	    };
56	    loop {
57	        // No file ops yet (Phase 5): receive and drop application traffic. The
58	        // SEF framework still services control traffic (pings/signals/re-init)
59	        // inside `receive`; only the application messages it hands back are
60	        // discarded here.
61	        let _ = sef.receive(&mut msg);
62	    }
63	}
64	
65	/// SEF fresh-init callback: publish VFS's endpoint to DS under its name. DS
66	/// registers the caller's kernel-stamped endpoint, so `_endpoint` from
67	/// `GET_WHOAMI` is not sent.
68	#[cfg_attr(test, allow(dead_code))]
69	fn vfs_init(_endpoint: Endpoint, name: &[u8; SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN]) -> i32 {
70	    sef_publish_to_ds(name)
71	}
72	
73	// The freestanding panic handler; under `cargo test` std supplies its own.
74	#[cfg(not(test))]
75	#[panic_handler]
76	fn panic(_: &core::panic::PanicInfo) -> ! {
77	    loop {
78	        core::hint::spin_loop()
79	    }
80	}
81

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * minix.rs user-space linker script (aarch64 EL0 servers).
3	 *
4	 * Produces a static ET_EXEC ELF that the kernel's minimal loader
5	 * (`kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`) maps segment-by-segment into a fresh
6	 * per-process AddrSpace. The loader is deliberately tiny, so this script
7	 * keeps every PT_LOAD page-aligned (vaddr *and* file offset) and emits no
8	 * dynamic sections / relocations.
9	 *
10	 * Load base 0x0010_0000 (1 MiB): comfortably below USER_VA_TOP and clear of
11	 * the kernel's hand-coded EL0 stub VAs (code 0x40_0000-0x43_0000, stacks
12	 * 0x80_0000-0x83_0000, stub-D heap 0x0100_0000). The stack is NOT part of
13	 * the ELF — the kernel allocates a stack frame and sets SP_EL0 before eret.
14	 * Each server gets its own per-process TTBR0, so this shared load base
15	 * never collides across servers.
16	 */
17	
18	OUTPUT_FORMAT(elf64-littleaarch64)
19	OUTPUT_ARCH(aarch64)
20	ENTRY(_start)
21	
22	PHDRS {
23	    text   PT_LOAD FLAGS(5); /* R+X */
24	    rodata PT_LOAD FLAGS(4); /* R   */
25	    data   PT_LOAD FLAGS(6); /* RW  */
26	}
27	
28	SECTIONS {
29	    . = 0x00100000;
30	
31	    .text : ALIGN(4K) {
32	        KEEP(*(.text._start))
33	        *(.text .text.*)
34	    } :text
35	
36	    . = ALIGN(4K);
37	    .rodata : ALIGN(4K) {
38	        *(.rodata .rodata.*)
39	    } :rodata
40	
41	    . = ALIGN(4K);
42	    .data : ALIGN(4K) {
43	        *(.data .data.*)
44	    } :data
45	
46	    .bss : ALIGN(4K) {
47	        *(.bss .bss.*)
48	        *(COMMON)
49	    } :data
50	
51	    /DISCARD/ : {
52	        *(.eh_frame)
53	        *(.eh_frame_hdr)
54	        *(.note .note.*)
55	        *(.comment)
56	        *(.dynsym .dynstr .hash .gnu.hash .dynamic)
57	    }
58	}
59

1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
2	// Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors
3	//! minix.rs PM (process manager) server — part A (slice 4.5).
4	//!
5	//! PM owns the POSIX-visible process identity the kernel deliberately knows
6	//! nothing about: pids, parentage, and signal disposition. Part A stands up
7	//! the [`mproc`] table, `getpid`/`getppid` (one `PM_GETPID` call — the reply's
8	//! `m_type` is the pid, payload `0..4` the parent's pid), and the minimal
9	//! kill path: PM drains kernel-queued signals and, with no handlers or masks
10	//! in Phase 4, applies the default disposition — terminate. Fork/exec/wait
11	//! are part B (slice 4.6).
12	//!
13	//! ## The kill chain (all observable kernel-side; EL0 cannot print)
14	//!
15	//! A system process raises a signal with `SYS_KILL` (in 4.5: VM, when a page
16	//! fault lands outside every region — stub D's deliberate SIGSEGV). The
17	//! kernel queues it (`cause_sig`) and wakes PM with a `NOTIFY` from `SYSTEM`;
18	//! [`drain_ksigs`] then loops `SYS_GETKSIG` → disposition → `SYS_ENDKSIG`
19	//! until the kernel reports no more pending procs. Boot servers
20	//! (`MF_PRIV_PROC`) are never terminated — signal-as-message delivery to
21	//! system processes (MINIX's sig2mess) waits for a consumer (RS restarts).
22	//! Every `SYS_GETKSIG`-returned target is `SYS_ENDKSIG`-acknowledged, even
23	//! skipped ones, so no proc is left stranded in signal-pending state; the
24	//! terminate (`SYS_EXIT`) happens *before* the acknowledge so the target
25	//! never becomes runnable in between.
26	//!
27	//! ## Live demo
28	//!
29	//! PM's SEF init also releases stub E — built frozen (`RTS_NO_PRIV`, no priv
30	//! slot) at boot — via `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)`, standing in for the
31	//! fork path that will create frozen children in 4.6. E then exercises
32	//! `PM_GETPID` in a SENDREC loop over the PM ↔ shared-USER-priv edge opened
33	//! by `populate_user_priv`.
34	
35	// Freestanding for the bare-metal build, but a normal host binary under
36	// `cargo test` so `mproc`'s logic gets host-runnable unit tests. Same gating
37	// as the RS/SCHED servers.
38	#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)]
39	#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_main)]
40	
41	mod mproc;
42	
43	use minixrs_ipc::{ipc_send, ipc_sendrec};
44	use minixrs_kernel_shared::Message;
45	use minixrs_kernel_shared::callnr::{
46	    PM_EXIT, PM_FORK, PM_GETPID, PM_WAIT, PRIVCTL_SET_USER, SCHEDULING_START, SCHEDULING_STOP,
47	    SYS_ENDKSIG, SYS_EXIT, SYS_FORK, SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN, SYS_GETKSIG, SYS_PRIVCTL, VM_FORK,
48	};
49	use minixrs_kernel_shared::com::{
50	    SCHED_PROC_NR, STUB_E_PROC_NR, SYSTEM, VM_PROC_NR, boot_endpoint,
51	};
52	use minixrs_kernel_shared::endpoint::{Endpoint, NONE, endpoint_proc};
53	use minixrs_kernel_shared::error::{EAGAIN, ECHILD, EINVAL, ESRCH, OK};
54	use minixrs_kernel_shared::ipc_const::NOTIFY_MESSAGE;
55	use minixrs_server_rt::{SefConfig, sef_publish_to_ds, sef_startup};
56	
57	/// Priority band and quantum PM assigns a forked child via `SCHEDULING_START`.
58	/// These match SCHED's own `USER_Q` / `QUANTUM` (`servers/sched/src/policy.rs`)
59	/// so a child round-robins in the managed band rather than sinking behind the
60	/// kernel-scheduled stubs.
61	const CHILD_PRIORITY: i32 = 8;
62	const CHILD_QUANTUM: i32 = 5;
63	
64	/// ELF entry point. The kernel primes `SP_EL0` before `eret`, so `_start`
65	/// dives straight into Rust. Gated to `not(test)` (under `cargo test` the
66	/// crate links as a host binary with the C runtime's `_start`);
67	/// `.text._start` is gated to the bare-metal target so a Mach-O host
68	/// `cargo check` still type-checks.
69	#[cfg(not(test))]
70	#[unsafe(no_mangle)]
71	#[cfg_attr(target_os = "none", unsafe(link_section = ".text._start"))]
72	pub extern "C" fn _start() -> ! {
73	    main()
74	}
75	
76	#[cfg_attr(test, allow(dead_code))]
77	fn main() -> ! {
78	    // `sef_startup` learns PM's endpoint/name via SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI) and
79	    // runs `pm_init` (DS publish, mproc seed, stub E release). No signal
80	    // handler: PM *is* the signal manager, not a signal consumer. On failure
81	    // there is nothing to print from EL0 — park forever.
82	    let sef = sef_startup(SefConfig {
83	        init_fresh: Some(pm_init),
84	        signal_handler: None,
85	    })
86	    .unwrap_or_else(|_| {
87	        loop {
88	            core::hint::spin_loop()
89	        }
90	    });
91	
92	    let system = boot_endpoint(SYSTEM);
93	    // VM and SCHED are boot servers at generation 0, so `boot_endpoint` is their
94	    // live endpoint (they never exit in Phase 4). PM drives the fork tree
95	    // through them: VM clones the child's regions, SCHED schedules it.
96	    let vm = boot_endpoint(VM_PROC_NR);
97	    let sched = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR);
98	
99	    let mut msg = Message {
100	        m_source: 0,
101	        m_type: 0,
102	        payload: [0u8; 96],
103	    };
104	    loop {
105	        if sef.receive(&mut msg) != 0 {
106	            continue;
107	        }
108	        match msg.m_type {
109	            // The kernel's ksig wake-up (`deliver_ksig`): a NOTIFY stamped
110	            // from SYSTEM. RS heartbeat pings are also NOTIFYs but are
111	            // consumed inside `sef.receive` (classified `Ping`), so a NOTIFY
112	            // from any other source here is simply ignored.
113	            NOTIFY_MESSAGE if msg.m_source == system => drain_ksigs(system),
114	            PM_GETPID => handle_getpid(&mut msg),
115	            PM_FORK => handle_fork(system, vm, sched, &mut msg),
116	            PM_EXIT => handle_exit(system, sched, &mut msg),
117	            PM_WAIT => handle_wait(&mut msg),
118	            // Unknown request: drop it.
119	            _ => {}
120	        }
121	    }
122	}
123	
124	/// SEF fresh-init callback: publish PM's endpoint to DS, seed the mproc
125	/// table, and release the frozen stub E onto the shared USER priv slot.

1	/* SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause */
2	/* Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors */
3	/*
4	 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
5	 *
6	 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
7	 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
8	 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
9	 *
10	 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
11	 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
12	 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
13	 *
14	 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
15	 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
16	 *
17	 *   Stub C — kernel-call client (slice 2.6): SENDREC to the SYSTEM
18	 *   endpoint with m_type = SYS_GETINFO and payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI in
19	 *   a loop. The kernel dispatcher writes endpoint, priv-flags, init-flags,
20	 *   and name back into the payload; the reply's m_type is OK (0).
21	 *
22	 *   Stub D — page-fault demo (slice 3.4b): touches an unmapped heap page at
23	 *   0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first store faults; the kernel routes a
24	 *   VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server, which maps the page via
25	 *   SYS_VMCTL and clears D's fault, after which the store retries and
26	 *   succeeds. Later iterations hit the now-mapped page with no further
27	 *   faults. D issues no SVC of its own.
28	 *
29	 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
30	 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
31	 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
32	 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
33	 *
34	 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11, 12, and
35	 * the SYSTEM kernel task (-2). make_endpoint(0, p) = p & 0x7FFF, so
36	 * SYSTEM at gen 0 = (-2 & 0x7FFF) = 0x7FFE. ANY's encoding is
37	 * `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
38	 */
39	
40	    .equ SEND,         1
41	    .equ RECEIVE,      2
42	    .equ SENDREC,      3
43	    .equ ANY,          0x3FFF
44	    .equ ENDPOINT_A,   11
45	    .equ ENDPOINT_B,   12
46	    .equ ENDPOINT_PM,  0                   // boot_endpoint(PM_PROC_NR = 0)
47	    .equ ENDPOINT_VM,  7                   // boot_endpoint(VM_PROC_NR = 7)
48	    .equ ENDPOINT_SYS, 0x7FFE              // boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)
49	    .equ SYS_GETINFO,  0x600               // KERNEL_CALL + 0
50	    .equ GET_WHOAMI,   12
51	    .equ PM_GETPID,    0x700               // PM_RQ_BASE + 0
52	    .equ PM_FORK,      0x701               // PM_RQ_BASE + 1
53	    .equ PM_EXIT,      0x702               // PM_RQ_BASE + 2
54	    .equ PM_WAIT,      0x703               // PM_RQ_BASE + 3
55	    .equ VM_BRK,       0xC01               // VM_RQ_BASE + 1
56	    .equ VM_MMAP,      0xC02               // VM_RQ_BASE + 2
57	    .equ VM_MUNMAP,    0xC03               // VM_RQ_BASE + 3
58	    .equ MSG_SIZE,     104                 // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12
59	
60	/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

file.content:
/* SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause */
/* Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors */
/*
 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
 *
 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
 *
 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
 *
 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
 *
 *   Stub C — kernel-call client (slice 2.6): SENDREC to the SYSTEM
 *   endpoint with m_type = SYS_GETINFO and payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI in
 *   a loop. The kernel dispatcher writes endpoint, priv-flags, init-flags,
 *   and name back into the payload; the reply's m_type is OK (0).
 *
 *   Stub D — page-fault demo (slice 3.4b): touches an unmapped heap page at
 *   0x0100_0000 in a loop. The first store faults; the kernel routes a
 *   VM_PAGEFAULT to the user-space VM server, which maps the page via
 *   SYS_VMCTL and clears D's fault, after which the store retries and
 *   succeeds. Later iterations hit the now-mapped page with no further
 *   faults. D issues no SVC of its own.
 *
 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
 *
 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11, 12, and
 * the SYSTEM kernel task (-2). make_endpoint(0, p) = p & 0x7FFF, so
 * SYSTEM at gen 0 = (-2 & 0x7FFF) = 0x7FFE. ANY's encoding is
 * `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
 */

    .equ SEND,         1
    .equ RECEIVE,      2
    .equ SENDREC,      3
    .equ ANY,          0x3FFF
    .equ ENDPOINT_A,   11
    .equ ENDPOINT_B,   12
    .equ ENDPOINT_PM,  0                   // boot_endpoint(PM_PROC_NR = 0)
    .equ ENDPOINT_VM,  7                   // boot_endpoint(VM_PROC_NR = 7)
    .equ ENDPOINT_SYS, 0x7FFE              // boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)
    .equ SYS_GETINFO,  0x600               // KERNEL_CALL + 0
    .equ GET_WHOAMI,   12
    .equ PM_GETPID,    0x700               // PM_RQ_BASE + 0
    .equ PM_FORK,      0x701               // PM_RQ_BASE + 1
    .equ PM_EXIT,      0x702               // PM_RQ_BASE + 2
    .equ PM_WAIT,      0x703               // PM_RQ_BASE + 3
    .equ VM_BRK,       0xC01               // VM_RQ_BASE + 1
    .equ VM_MMAP,      0xC02               // VM_RQ_BASE + 2
    .equ VM_MUNMAP,    0xC03               // VM_RQ_BASE + 3
    .equ MSG_SIZE,     104                 // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12

/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

content.text: Now rewrite stub E's child branch (label `3:`) from `PM_EXIT` to `PM_EXEC`, and update the block comment:

input.old_string:
*   loop:
 *     SENDREC(PM, PM_FORK)              // fork() — returns twice
 *     if reply m_type == 0:            //   child half
 *         SENDREC(PM, PM_EXIT, 0)      //   exit(0); PM tears us down, no return
 *     else:                            //   parent half (m_type = child pid)
 *         SENDREC(PM, PM_WAIT)         //   wait() for the child, then loop
 *
 * The child is a byte-for-byte copy of E's frame taken mid-fork SENDREC, so
 * both halves resume here reading the same reply buffer; only the reply m_type
 * (0 vs the child pid) tells them apart. Observable kernel-side via
 * [fork]/[exit] traces and the SYS_FORK/SYS_EXIT [ksys] samples.
 */
input.new_string:
*   loop:
 *     SENDREC(PM, PM_FORK)              // fork() — returns twice
 *     if reply m_type == 0:            //   child half
 *         SENDREC(PM, PM_EXEC)         //   execve("worker"); replaces our image
 *     else:                            //   parent half (m_type = child pid)
 *         SENDREC(PM, PM_WAIT)         //   wait() for the child, then loop
 *
 * The child is a byte-for-byte copy of E's frame taken mid-fork SENDREC, so
 * both halves resume here reading the same reply buffer; only the reply m_type
 * (0 vs the child pid) tells them apart. The child then execs the boot-embedded
 * `worker` binary (slice 4.7): PM issues SYS_EXEC, the kernel replaces the
 * child's image, and it resumes at worker's _start — so the PM_EXEC SENDREC
 * never returns here. The worker runs a few getpids then exits, the parent
 * reaps it, and the loop forks again reusing the same child slot. Observable
 * kernel-side via the SYS_FORK/SYS_EXEC/SYS_EXIT [ksys] samples.
 */

toolUseResult.newString:
*   loop:
 *     SENDREC(PM, PM_FORK)              // fork() — returns twice
 *     if reply m_type == 0:            //   child half
 *         SENDREC(PM, PM_EXEC)         //   execve("worker"); replaces our image
 *     else:                            //   parent half (m_type = child pid)
 *         SENDREC(PM, PM_WAIT)         //   wait() for the child, then loop
 *
 * The child is a byte-for-byte copy of E's frame taken mid-fork SENDREC, so
 * both halves resume here reading the same reply buffer; only the reply m_type
 * (0 vs the child pid) tells them apart. The child then execs the boot-embedded
 * `worker` binary (slice 4.7): PM issues SYS_EXEC, the kernel replaces the
 * child's image, and it resumes at worker's _start — so the PM_EXEC SENDREC
 * never returns here. The worker runs a few getpids then exits, the parent
 * reaps it, and the loop forks again reusing the same child slot. Observable
 * kernel-side via the SYS_FORK/SYS_EXEC/SYS_EXIT [ksys] samples.
 */

content.text: The exec cycle works exactly as designed: fork child 16 → child execs `worker` (old image freed, ASID recycled 12↔10) → worker runs and exits as `nr=16` renamed `w` → parent reaps → loops. 6 forks / 6 execs / 6 exits all matched, endpoint generation advancing (`0x10 → 0x8010 → 0x10010 → 0x18010`). Now confirm the worker's getpid IPC and that all regressions are intact.

content.text:
QEMU verification is a complete success:

- **11 boot `[as]`** lines — worker correctly **absent** (exec-only, skipped by the loader)
- **6× fork→exec→worker→exit→reap** cycles, all reusing child slot 16 with **advancing endpoint generation** (`0x10→0x8010→0x10010→0x18010`) and **ASID recycling** (12↔10)
- `SYS_EXEC` frees the old image (`freed=2`), loads `worker` (`entry=0x100000`), renames the proc (`SYS_EXIT target=w`)
- Worker's `PM_GETPID` round-trips to PM (`caller=16 target=0x0`)
- **All regressions intact**: A↔B, C `SYS_GETINFO`, D's 3 `[pf]` + SIGSEGV kill, SCHED `[noq]`, 6 RS `[alarm]` fires
- **Zero** panics / `el0_sync_unexpected`; every `result=0`

Now the final task — coverage exclusion and docs. Let me update sonar-project.properties.

content.text: Now docs/plan.md. Let me re-read the exact 4.6b / 4.7 / 4.8 marker text.

content.content:
997	- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait (two PRs like 3.4). **4.6a**
998	  ✓ shipped (PR #28, merged 2026-07-18): the kernel half — `SYS_FORK` (free
999	  slot, copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by
1000	  walking the parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred;
1001	  zeroes `sig_pending` on slot reuse), completion of 4.5's `SYS_EXIT`-lite into a
1002	  full teardown (`AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot, bump generation,
1003	  `unblock_dependents`), `okendpt` stale-generation rejection, and ASID
1004	  free-list recycling. **4.6b** ◀ ready (branch
1005	  `feature/phase-4-6b-pm-fork-exit-wait`, pending merge): the PM/VM/stub-E half.
1006	  New PM requests `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) let a user
1007	  proc drive the lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape: user → PM, never
1008	  user → kernel). `handle_fork` owns the tree — allocate a child `mproc` slot
1009	  (fork pool `[16, NR_MPROCS)`, slot index = child kernel proc-nr), `SYS_FORK`
1010	  (kernel clones the *frozen* child), `VM_FORK` (new `0xC04` request; VM's
1011	  `region::fork` clones the parent's `ClientRegions`, `MAX_CLIENTS` widened
1012	  16→32 to address the fork pool), `SCHEDULING_START` (SCHED schedules the
1013	  still-frozen child — verified safe: `rts_unset` only enqueues on the
1014	  last-block-bit clear, so `SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_PRIVCTL` leave it a blocked
1015	  `RTS_RECEIVING` receiver), `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` to release the
1016	  freeze, then a reply to **both** halves of the shared SENDREC (child gets
1017	  `m_type = 0`, parent gets the child pid — MINIX fork-returns-twice). `mproc`
1018	  gains a generation-aware `endpoint` field, `exit_status`, `MF_WAITING`, and a
1019	  free-slot allocator / zombie-and-reap helpers (all pure `*_in`, host-tested).
1020	  `handle_exit` = `SCHEDULING_STOP` (before teardown, while the endpoint is
1021	  valid) + `SYS_EXIT` + zombie; `handle_wait` reaps a zombie or suspends the
1022	  parent (no reply) until `handle_exit` wakes it. **Scope decision:** parent
1023	  notification is the zombie + wait-reap handshake only — the kernel signal path
1024	  default-*terminates* user procs, so a real `SIGCHLD` to the handler-less
1025	  parent would kill it; async `SIGCHLD` waits for Phase 5 handlers. No new priv
1026	  wiring (PM↔VM, PM↔SCHED, child↔`USER_PRIV_ID` edges all pre-exist). Stub E
1027	  rewritten from the 4.5 `PM_GETPID` loop into a fork/exit/wait loop: fork, and
1028	  on the reply `m_type` branch child→`PM_EXIT(0)` vs parent→`PM_WAIT`→loop.
1029	  Verified in QEMU over 25 s: eleven `[as]` lines; `[ksys SYS_PRIVCTL] target=E`
1030	  releasing E then each child; a sustained fork loop (69 cycles, still forking at
1031	  t≈25 s) all reusing child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint
1032	  generation (`0x10 → 0x220010` — proof of `okendpt` slot/ASID recycling and
1033	  full reap between cycles), `[ksys SYS_FORK]`/`SYS_EXIT] target=E nr=16 freed=2`
1034	  round-trips; A↔B ping-pong, C `SYS_GETINFO`, D's 3 resolved `[pf]` + 1 fatal
1035	  SIGSEGV, six RS `[alarm]` fires, SCHED `[noq]` delegation all intact; every
1036	  traced `result=0` (bar D's designed kill), zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
1037	  Host: `cargo test --workspace` green (new `mproc` fork/wait, `region::fork`,
1038	  `callnr` contiguity tests); `cargo check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` +
1039	  fmt clean.
1040	- **Slice 4.7** ◀ next — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
1041	  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
1042	  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
1043	  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
1044	  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
1045	  target.
1046	- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**
1047	  init (a freestanding Rust ELF loaded into `INIT_PROC_NR=10` by the archive)
1048	  forks children that exec the worker, `wait`s, and respawns in a loop. Retire or
1049	  demote the A/B/C/D stubs now that init + real procs are the live exercise. Real
1050	  mdBook *Servers* chapter (`book/src/servers/overview.md`); CLAUDE.md Phase-4
1051	  conventions; flip every slice marker and write the Phase-4-complete aggregate
1052	  paragraph.

- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait (two PRs like 3.4). **4.6a**
  ✓ shipped (PR #28, merged 2026-07-18): the kernel half — `SYS_FORK` (free
  slot, copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by
  walking the parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred;
  zeroes `sig_pending` on slot reuse), completion of 4.5's `SYS_EXIT`-lite into a
  full teardown (`AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot, bump generation,
  `unblock_dependents`), `okendpt` stale-generation rejection, and ASID
  free-list recycling. **4.6b** ◀ ready (branch
  `feature/phase-4-6b-pm-fork-exit-wait`, pending merge): the PM/VM/stub-E half.
  New PM requests `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) let a user
  proc drive the lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape: user → PM, never
  user → kernel). `handle_fork` owns the tree — allocate a child `mproc` slot
  (fork pool `[16, NR_MPROCS)`, slot index = child kernel proc-nr), `SYS_FORK`
  (kernel clones the *frozen* child), `VM_FORK` (new `0xC04` request; VM's
  `region::fork` clones the parent's `ClientRegions`, `MAX_CLIENTS` widened
  16→32 to address the fork pool), `SCHEDULING_START` (SCHED schedules the
  still-frozen child — verified safe: `rts_unset` only enqueues on the
  last-block-bit clear, so `SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_PRIVCTL` leave it a blocked
  `RTS_RECEIVING` receiver), `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` to release the
  freeze, then a reply to **both** halves of the shared SENDREC (child gets
  `m_type = 0`, parent gets the child pid — MINIX fork-returns-twice). `mproc`
  gains a generation-aware `endpoint` field, `exit_status`, `MF_WAITING`, and a
  free-slot allocator / zombie-and-reap helpers (all pure `*_in`, host-tested).
  `handle_exit` = `SCHEDULING_STOP` (before teardown, while the endpoint is
  valid) + `SYS_EXIT` + zombie; `handle_wait` reaps a zombie or suspends the
  parent (no reply) until `handle_exit` wakes it. **Scope decision:** parent
  notification is the zombie + wait-reap handshake only — the kernel signal path
  default-*terminates* user procs, so a real `SIGCHLD` to the handler-less
  parent would kill it; async `SIGCHLD` waits for Phase 5 handlers. No new priv
  wiring (PM↔VM, PM↔SCHED, child↔`USER_PRIV_ID` edges all pre-exist). Stub E
  rewritten from the 4.5 `PM_GETPID` loop into a fork/exit/wait loop: fork, and
  on the reply `m_type` branch child→`PM_EXIT(0)` vs parent→`PM_WAIT`→loop.
  Verified in QEMU over 25 s: eleven `[as]` lines; `[ksys SYS_PRIVCTL] target=E`
  releasing E then each child; a sustained fork loop (69 cycles, still forking at
  t≈25 s) all reusing child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint
  generation (`0x10 → 0x220010` — proof of `okendpt` slot/ASID recycling and
  full reap between cycles), `[ksys SYS_FORK]`/`SYS_EXIT] target=E nr=16 freed=2`
  round-trips; A↔B ping-pong, C `SYS_GETINFO`, D's 3 resolved `[pf]` + 1 fatal
  SIGSEGV, six RS `[alarm]` fires, SCHED `[noq]` delegation all intact; every
  traced `result=0` (bar D's designed kill), zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
  Host: `cargo test --workspace` green (new `mproc` fork/wait, `region::fork`,
  `callnr` contiguity tests); `cargo check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` +
  fmt clean.
- **Slice 4.7** ◀ next — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
  target.
- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**
  init (a freestanding Rust ELF loaded into `INIT_PROC_NR=10` by the archive)
  forks children that exec the worker, `wait`s, and respawns in a loop. Retire or
  demote the A/B/C/D stubs now that init + real procs are the live exercise. Real
  mdBook *Servers* chapter (`book/src/servers/overview.md`); CLAUDE.md Phase-4
  conventions; flip every slice marker and write the Phase-4-complete aggregate
  paragraph.

  allow now dropped), builds a fresh address space via a new arch helper

input.description: Locate the 4.6b convention bullet in CLAUDE.md

content.content: 133:- PM-driven fork/exit/wait (slice 4.6b): user procs drive the process lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape — user → PM, never user → kernel; the shared `USER_PRIV_ID` only opens `ipc_to = {PM}`), via `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) and `VM_FORK` (`VM_RQ_BASE + 4 = 0xC04`). PM's `handle_fork` owns the whole tree in a **fixed order that the frozen-child invariant depends on**: alloc an `mproc` child slot (fork pool `[FORK_POOL_BASE = NR_BOOT_PROCS+NR_STUB_PROCS = 16, NR_MPROCS = 32)`; slot index *is* the child's kernel proc-nr) → `SYS_FORK(parent_e, child_nr)` → `VM_FORK(parent_e, child_e)` → `SCHEDULING_START` → `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` release → reply to **both** halves of the shared SENDREC (child `m_type = 0`, parent `m_type = child_pid`; MINIX fork-returns-twice). Why the order is safe: `do_fork` creates the child `RTS_RECEIVING | RTS_NO_PRIV` (frozen), and `sched::rts_unset` only enqueues on the *last* block bit clearing — so `SYS_SCHEDULE` (via `SCHEDULING_START`) and `SYS_PRIVCTL` (clears `RTS_NO_PRIV`, leaving `RTS_RECEIVING`) both leave the child a blocked receiver off the run queue; **only PM's reply** (clearing `RTS_RECEIVING`) makes it runnable, so it can't run before its identity/memory/scheduling are built. On any mid-fork failure PM rolls back at **every** step — `SYS_FORK`/`VM_FORK`/`SCHEDULING_START`/`SYS_PRIVCTL` each check their result and, on error, `SYS_EXIT` the child + `mproc::cleanup` the slot before returning the errno to the parent (the post-`SCHEDULING_START` rollback does `SCHEDULING_STOP` first, endpoint-still-valid, mirroring `handle_exit`); the last two are boot-server-backed and can't fail with correct wiring, so their guards are defense-in-depth against ever recording an unrunnable child (which would hang the parent's `wait()`). `handle_exit` = `SCHEDULING_STOP` **before** `SYS_EXIT` (once `SYS_EXIT` bumps the generation, `okendpt` rejects the endpoint) + mark the `mproc` slot a zombie holding the encoded status (`W_EXITCODE = (status & 0xff) << 8`); PM sends the dead child **no** reply. `handle_wait` reaps a zombie child (reply pid + status, `cleanup` the slot) or, with a live child, sets `MF_WAITING` and **suspends** the parent (no reply) until `handle_exit` wakes it directly. **No async `SIGCHLD`** — the kernel signal path default-*terminates*, which would kill the handler-less parent stub, so parent-notify is the zombie + wait-reap handshake only (async signals wait for Phase 5 handlers). `mproc` (`servers/pm/src/mproc.rs`) now stores a generation-aware `endpoint` per slot (`boot_endpoint(slot)` for seeded procs, the `SYS_FORK` reply for children) + `exit_status` + `MF_WAITING`, with the free-slot allocator / zombie / reap logic in pure host-tested `*_in` helpers. VM's `region.rs` `MAX_CLIENTS` is widened 16 → 32 so the fork pool's child proc-nrs are addressable, and `region::fork(parent_nr, child_nr)` copies the whole `ClientRegions` (a `Copy` snapshot). **No new priv wiring** — PM↔VM and PM↔SCHED are boot-server `[0,n_active)` edges, child↔PM is the `USER_PRIV_ID` edge. Stub E is the live demo (fork/exit/wait loop; branches on the fork reply `m_type`: child → `PM_EXIT`, parent → `PM_WAIT` → loop). Recycling proof in QEMU: every fork reuses child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint generation, so a fresh fork implies the prior child was torn down *and* reaped

content.subject: kernel-shared: PM_EXEC + EXEC_NAME_LEN + test
content.description: callnr.rs: add PM_EXEC=0x704, bump NR_PM_MSGS to 5, add EXEC_NAME_LEN=16, extend pm_msgs_contiguous_from_base test. Optionally EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR in com.rs.

content.subject: kernel: do_exec.rs + dispatch + arch helper
content.description: New system/do_exec.rs (target-taking SYS_EXEC); route it in mod.rs beside SYS_FORK; remove caller-local arm + stubs::do_exec; make teardown_addrspace pub(super); add userland::load_exec_image + expose STUB_SPSR_EL0; drop dead_code on module_by_name.

content.subject: worker ELF crate
content.description: New userland/worker crate: Cargo.toml, user.ld, src/main.rs (getpid loop + PM_EXIT). Add to workspace members.

content.subject: PM handle_exec + sys_exec helper
content.description: servers/pm/src/main.rs: PM_EXEC dispatch arm, handle_exec, sys_exec helper, EXEC_TARGET const.

content.subject: Demo wiring: stub E exec + build.rs pack + boot skip
content.description: user_stub.S: stub E child does PM_EXEC. build.rs: pack worker with proc_nr -1. userland.rs boot loop: skip nr<0. Update stale comments.

content.subject: Coverage + docs + verify
content.description: sonar-project.properties userland exclusion; docs/plan.md markers + 4.7 paragraph; CLAUDE.md note. Then cargo test/fmt/clippy + cargo kernel-aarch64 + QEMU 25s verification.

lastPrompt: Work on the next slice in @docs/plan.md

content.text: Let me read the line after the 4.6b bullet to anchor the insertion.

content.content:
133	- PM-driven fork/exit/wait (slice 4.6b): user procs drive the process lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape — user → PM, never user → kernel; the shared `USER_PRIV_ID` only opens `ipc_to = {PM}`), via `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) and `VM_FORK` (`VM_RQ_BASE + 4 = 0xC04`). PM's `handle_fork` owns the whole tree in a **fixed order that the frozen-child invariant depends on**: alloc an `mproc` child slot (fork pool `[FORK_POOL_BASE = NR_BOOT_PROCS+NR_STUB_PROCS = 16, NR_MPROCS = 32)`; slot index *is* the child's kernel proc-nr) → `SYS_FORK(parent_e, child_nr)` → `VM_FORK(parent_e, child_e)` → `SCHEDULING_START` → `SYS_PRIVCTL(PRIVCTL_SET_USER)` release → reply to **both** halves of the shared SENDREC (child `m_type = 0`, parent `m_type = child_pid`; MINIX fork-returns-twice). Why the order is safe: `do_fork` creates the child `RTS_RECEIVING | RTS_NO_PRIV` (frozen), and `sched::rts_unset` only enqueues on the *last* block bit clearing — so `SYS_SCHEDULE` (via `SCHEDULING_START`) and `SYS_PRIVCTL` (clears `RTS_NO_PRIV`, leaving `RTS_RECEIVING`) both leave the child a blocked receiver off the run queue; **only PM's reply** (clearing `RTS_RECEIVING`) makes it runnable, so it can't run before its identity/memory/scheduling are built. On any mid-fork failure PM rolls back at **every** step — `SYS_FORK`/`VM_FORK`/`SCHEDULING_START`/`SYS_PRIVCTL` each check their result and, on error, `SYS_EXIT` the child + `mproc::cleanup` the slot before returning the errno to the parent (the post-`SCHEDULING_START` rollback does `SCHEDULING_STOP` first, endpoint-still-valid, mirroring `handle_exit`); the last two are boot-server-backed and can't fail with correct wiring, so their guards are defense-in-depth against ever recording an unrunnable child (which would hang the parent's `wait()`). `handle_exit` = `SCHEDULING_STOP` **before** `SYS_EXIT` (once `SYS_EXIT` bumps the generation, `okendpt` rejects the endpoint) + mark the `mproc` slot a zombie holding the encoded status (`W_EXITCODE = (status & 0xff) << 8`); PM sends the dead child **no** reply. `handle_wait` reaps a zombie child (reply pid + status, `cleanup` the slot) or, with a live child, sets `MF_WAITING` and **suspends** the parent (no reply) until `handle_exit` wakes it directly. **No async `SIGCHLD`** — the kernel signal path default-*terminates*, which would kill the handler-less parent stub, so parent-notify is the zombie + wait-reap handshake only (async signals wait for Phase 5 handlers). `mproc` (`servers/pm/src/mproc.rs`) now stores a generation-aware `endpoint` per slot (`boot_endpoint(slot)` for seeded procs, the `SYS_FORK` reply for children) + `exit_status` + `MF_WAITING`, with the free-slot allocator / zombie / reap logic in pure host-tested `*_in` helpers. VM's `region.rs` `MAX_CLIENTS` is widened 16 → 32 so the fork pool's child proc-nrs are addressable, and `region::fork(parent_nr, child_nr)` copies the whole `ClientRegions` (a `Copy` snapshot). **No new priv wiring** — PM↔VM and PM↔SCHED are boot-server `[0,n_active)` edges, child↔PM is the `USER_PRIV_ID` edge. Stub E is the live demo (fork/exit/wait loop; branches on the fork reply `m_type`: child → `PM_EXIT`, parent → `PM_WAIT` → loop). Recycling proof in QEMU: every fork reuses child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint generation, so a fresh fork implies the prior child was torn down *and* reaped
134	- QEMU trace forensics: the `[ipc]` modulo sampler almost never catches low-rate callers — a blocking SENDREC client (e.g. stub E's fork/wait loop) round-trips once per band-8 rotation, thousands of times rarer than stub C's synchronous kernel-call flood — and a server packed late in the MXBI archive (PM) boots after C's flood starts, so its SEF handshake never lands in `TRACE_HEAD` either. Zero sampled lines for such a caller is NOT evidence it's stuck: verify via its downstream head-carve `[ksys …]` traces (e.g. `[ksys SYS_FORK]`/`SYS_EXIT` are head-carved at 6, so raise the head const temporarily to count real cycles), or a temporary unconditional `[DBG]` trace in `ipc::do_ipc` keyed on the caller nr (remove before committing)
135	- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless
136	- `cargo test -p minixrs-kernel` runs zero tests by design — every kernel module is gated on `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` and host-test infra is not yet built; host-runnable tests live in `kernel-shared`. QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)

input.old_string: - QEMU trace forensics: the `[ipc]` modulo sampler almost never catches low-rate callers
input.new_string:
- PM-driven exec (slice 4.7): a user proc `SENDREC`s `PM_EXEC` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 4 = 0x704`, `NR_PM_MSGS` 4→5) to PM; PM issues `SYS_EXEC` naming that proc as the **target** (POSIX shape — exec is done *to* the caller, so `SYS_EXEC` moved from the caller-local arm to the target-taking `match` beside `SYS_FORK` in `kernel_call_dispatch`; `SYS_EXEC` was already numbered `0x603` and blanket-granted to PM by the SRV_T `k_call_mask` fill, so `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 18 and no priv wiring changes). `kernel/src/system/do_exec.rs`: resolve the target (reject `SELF`/self-target — the active-TTBR0 teardown hazard, the `do_exit` stance), read the binary name from payload `4..4+EXEC_NAME_LEN` (16, new in `callnr.rs`; MINIX-renames the proc to it — `EXEC_NAME_LEN <= PROC_NAME_LEN`), resolve it via `BootImage::module_by_name`, gate the target exactly like `do_fork`'s parent (a clean `RTS_RECEIVING` receiver — in the live flow mid-`SENDREC` to PM), build the new AS via `userland::load_exec_image`, reset the frame (`ArchRegisterFrame::EMPTY` + `elr_el1`/`sp_el0`/`spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0`), swap `(ttbr0_pa, asid)`, `do_exit::teardown_addrspace` (now `pub(super)`) the **old** image (safe: target ≠ caller), then `sched::rts_unset(RTS_RECEIVING)` to resume it at `_start`. exec preserves pid/priv/scheduler; the target gets **no reply** on success (kernel resumes it at the new entry), errno reply on failure — so PM's `handle_exec` replies only on `rc != OK`. `userland::load_exec_image(elf) -> Option<ExecImage>` is factored out of `load_boot_server` (`AddrSpace::new` + `elf::load_into` + one RW stack page at `SERVER_STACK_VA` + `alloc_asid`, `mem::forget` the tree; `None` + `destroy_addrspace_with_leaves` cleanup on OOM — the `do_fork` copy_addrspace no-leak contract). The exec target is a freestanding `userland/worker` ELF (getpid loop + `PM_EXIT`; **no** `server-rt`/SEF — a plain user program, deps `minix-ipc` + `kernel-shared` only), packed into the MXBI archive by `build.rs` with sentinel proc_nr `com::EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR = -1` — the boot loader (`userland.rs` load loop) skips any negative proc_nr, so `worker` is resolvable by name but **never boot-loaded** (no `[as]` line, no proc/priv slot). PM's `execve` hardcodes `EXEC_TARGET = "worker"` for the demo (a user-supplied path arrives with Phase-5 musl/filesystem; the kernel path is already name-driven). Stub E's child branch flips `PM_EXIT` → `PM_EXEC`: fork → child execs `worker` → worker exits → parent reaps → loop, so a `[ksys SYS_EXEC] target=16 name=worker` per cycle sits between the `SYS_FORK`/`SYS_EXIT nr=16` twins with a monotonically advancing endpoint generation + recycled ASID (exec teardown + reap proof). `userland/**/src/main.rs` added to `sonar.coverage.exclusions` (freestanding entry point, QEMU-verified, no host-testable logic — like the server `main.rs`es)
- QEMU trace forensics: the `[ipc]` modulo sampler almost never catches low-rate callers

toolUseResult.newString:

The exec target is a freestanding `userland/worker` ELF (getpid loop + `PM_EXIT`; **no** `server-rt`/SEF — a plain user program, deps `minix-ipc` + `kernel-shared` only), packed into the MXBI archive by `build.rs` with sentinel proc_nr `com::EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR = -1` — the boot loader (`userland.rs` load loop) skips any negative proc_nr, so `worker` is resolvable by name but **never boot-loaded** (no `[as]` line, no proc/priv slot). PM's `execve` hardcodes `EXEC_TARGET = "worker"` for the demo (a user-supplied path arrives with Phase-5 musl/filesystem; the kernel path is already name-driven). Stub E's child branch flips `PM_EXIT` → `PM_EXEC`: fork → child execs `worker` → worker exits → parent reaps → loop, so a `[ksys SYS_EXEC] target=16 name=worker` per cycle sits between the `SYS_FORK`/`SYS_EXIT nr=16` twins with a monotonically advancing endpoint generation + recycled ASID (exec teardown + reap proof). `userland/**/src/main.rs` added to `sonar.coverage.exclusions` (freestanding entry point, QEMU-verified, no host-testable logic — like the server `main.rs`es)
- QEMU trace forensics: the `[ipc]` modulo sampler almost never catches low-rate callers

lines: - Per-proc one-shot alarms (slice 4.4): `Proc::alarm_at` holds an absolute uptime tick (0 = disarmed); `clock::EARLIEST_ALARM` is an O(1) fast-path gate so `tick()` only pays the O(N) scan in `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` when one is due. Expiry delivers a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK` via `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm` — **no `ipc_to` check** (kernel-originated, like `mini_pf_send`; `CLOCK`'s `ipc_to` is empty so `mini_notify` would deny it), immediate if the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked else deferred via `notify_pending` against CLOCK's priv slot. A user-space periodic alarm is a re-arm per fire (RS). `SYS_SETALARM` (now real, caller-local) payload: relative `delta` ticks in `0..8` (0 cancels), reply = previous time-left; no new kernel-shared constants (reuses `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` + `CLOCK`). RS (`servers/rs`) heartbeats peers by `ipc_notify` (peers ack via `server-rt`'s SEF ping); RS keys its alarm on `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` (classified `Application`, not the RS-sourced SEF ping)

- Minimal signals (slice 4.5): the kernel half is `system/do_sig.rs` — `SYS_KILL` → `cause_sig` sets a bit in `Proc::sig_pending` (u32 bitmap; zeroed on slot free and again on fork's child populate) plus `RTS_SIGNALED|RTS_SIG_PENDING`, then wakes PM with `ipc::notify::deliver_ksig` (kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `SYSTEM`, no `ipc_to` check — the `deliver_alarm` pattern; `do_kill`'s deferred-notify write is why `kernel_call_dispatch` takes `&mut [Priv]`). Drain contract (amended in 4.6a): `SYS_GETKSIG` hands off the bitmap (its scan gates on `sig_pending != 0`, not the RTS bit alone, so a handed-off proc isn't re-returned) and PM disposes of every returned endpoint — `SYS_ENDKSIG` for survivors, `SYS_EXIT` (with **no** ENDKSIG after) for terminations, since 4.6's full exit zeroes signal state and frees the slot so a post-exit acknowledge just bounces off `okendpt` with `EDEADSRCDST`. `SYS_EXIT` is a **full teardown** as of 4.6a (`do_exit.rs`): stop + `caller_q` unlink as before, then `unblock_dependents` (every proc blocked SENDING-to/RECEIVING-from the dead endpoint resumes with `EDEADSRCDST` patched into its parked `x0`; dedicated-priv `notify_pending` bits purged, shared `USER_PRIV_ID` deliberately skipped), leaf frames freed via `addrspace::walk_leaves` + `flush_tlb_asid` + `AddrSpace::destroy` + `asid::free_asid` (ASIDs recycle through a free-list now), and `free_slot` (endpoint `bump_generation` — wraps to 1, never 0 — all per-proc state zeroed, `RTS_SLOT_FREE` stored). Every user-supplied-endpoint resolution goes through `table::okendpt` (stored-endpoint + not-free check → `EDEADSRCDST` on stale generations): `mini_send`/`mini_notify` dst, `mini_receive`'s non-ANY filter, and the target-taking kernel calls via `system::resolve_target` (`SELF` short-circuits first; `do_exit` rejects SELF and caller-named-self outright — tearing down the active TTBR0 mid-call is the hazard). `SYS_PRIVCTL` has one subcode, `PRIVCTL_SET_USER`: requires the target frozen on `RTS_NO_PRIV` (the freeze doubles as the authorization gate; EPERM on a live target) and points it at the shared `table::USER_PRIV_ID` = 20 (`USR_T`, `ipc_to` = {PM}, empty `k_call_mask`, `sig_mgr` = PM; `populate_user_priv` opens the reverse PM(priv 5)→20 `ipc_to` edge at boot). Frozen-stub pattern: `build_stub(nr, None, .., frozen: true)` = full AS built (ttbr0/asid set, satisfying `schedule_next`'s asserts), no priv, not enqueued — `PRIVCTL_SET_USER` releases it. PM's request range is `PM_RQ_BASE = 0x700` (the gap below VM `0xC00` — SCHED's `0xF00` was the last block under `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`); `PM_GETPID` replies `m_type` = pid (MINIX result-is-pid, errors negative — PM's own pid 0 is indistinguishable from `OK` by design) with ppid in payload `0..4`. Stub proc numbers are shared as `com::STUB_A..E_PROC_NR` (kernel `userland.rs` and PM's `mproc` seeding must agree; retired in 4.8). VM's out-of-region fault arm now raises `SYS_KILL(faulter, SIGSEGV)` instead of silently returning; the faulter stays `RTS_PAGEFAULT`-blocked until PM terminates it

- PM-driven fork/exit/wait (slice 4.6b): user procs drive the process lifecycle entirely through PM (POSIX shape — user → PM, never user → kernel; the shared `USER_PRIV_ID` only opens `ipc_to = {PM}`), via `PM_FORK`/`PM_EXIT`/`PM_WAIT` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 1..3`) and `VM_FORK` (`VM_RQ_BASE + 4 = 0xC04`). Stub E is the live demo (fork/exit/wait loop; branches on the fork reply `m_type`: child → `PM_EXIT`, parent → `PM_WAIT` → loop). Recycling proof in QEMU: every fork reuses child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint generation, so a fresh fork implies the prior child was torn down *and* reaped

+- PM-driven exec (slice 4.7): a user proc `SENDREC`s `PM_EXEC` (`PM_RQ_BASE + 4 = 0x704`, `NR_PM_MSGS` 4→5) to PM; PM issues `SYS_EXEC` naming that proc as the **target** (POSIX shape — exec is done *to* the caller, so `SYS_EXEC` moved from the caller-local arm to the target-taking `match` beside `SYS_FORK` in `kernel_call_dispatch`; `SYS_EXEC` was already numbered `0x603` and blanket-granted to PM by the SRV_T `k_call_mask` fill, so `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 18 and no priv wiring changes). `kernel/src/system/do_exec.rs`: resolve the target (reject `SELF`/self-target — the active-TTBR0 teardown hazard, the `do_exit` stance), read the binary name from payload `4..4+EXEC_NAME_LEN` (16, new in `callnr.rs`; MINIX-renames the proc to it — `EXEC_NAME_LEN <= PROC_NAME_LEN`), resolve it via `BootImage::module_by_name`, gate the target exactly like `do_fork`'s parent (a clean `RTS_RECEIVING` receiver — in the live flow mid-`SENDREC` to PM), build the new AS via `userland::load_exec_image`, reset the frame (`ArchRegisterFrame::EMPTY` + `elr_el1`/`sp_el0`/`spsr_el1 = STUB_SPSR_EL0`), swap `(ttbr0_pa, asid)`, `do_exit::teardown_addrspace` (now `pub(super)`) the **old** image (safe: target ≠ caller), then `sched::rts_unset(RTS_RECEIVING)` to resume it at `_start`. exec preserves pid/priv/scheduler; the target gets **no reply** on success (kernel resumes it at the new entry), errno reply on failure — so PM's `handle_exec` replies only on `rc != OK`. `userland::load_exec_image(elf) -> Option<ExecImage>` is factored out of `load_boot_server` (`AddrSpace::new` + `elf::load_into` + one RW stack page at `SERVER_STACK_VA` + `alloc_asid`, `mem::forget` the tree; `None` + `destroy_addrspace_with_leaves` cleanup on OOM — the `do_fork` copy_addrspace no-leak contract). The exec target is a freestanding `userland/worker` ELF (getpid loop + `PM_EXIT`; **no** `server-rt`/SEF — a plain user program, deps `minix-ipc` + `kernel-shared` only), packed into the MXBI archive by `build.rs` with sentinel proc_nr `com::EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR = -1` — the boot loader (`userland.rs` load loop) skips any negative proc_nr, so `worker` is resolvable by name but **never boot-loaded** (no `[as]` line, no proc/priv slot). PM's `execve` hardcodes `EXEC_TARGET = "worker"` for the demo (a user-supplied path arrives with Phase-5 musl/filesystem; the kernel path is already name-driven). Stub E's child branch flips `PM_EXIT` → `PM_EXEC`: fork → child execs `worker` → worker exits → parent reaps → loop, so a `[ksys SYS_EXEC] target=16 name=worker` per cycle sits between the `SYS_FORK`/`SYS_EXIT nr=16` twins with a monotonically advancing endpoint generation + recycled ASID (exec teardown + reap proof). `userland/**/src/main.rs` added to `sonar.coverage.exclusions` (freestanding entry point, QEMU-verified, no host-testable logic — like the server `main.rs`es)

- QEMU trace forensics: the `[ipc]` modulo sampler almost never catches low-rate callers — a blocking SENDREC client (e.g. stub E's fork/wait loop) round-trips once per band-8 rotation, thousands of times rarer than stub C's synchronous kernel-call flood — and a server packed late in the MXBI archive (PM) boots after C's flood starts, so its SEF handshake never lands in `TRACE_HEAD` either. Zero sampled lines for such a caller is NOT evidence it's stuck: verify via its downstream head-carve `[ksys …]` traces (e.g. `[ksys SYS_FORK]`/`SYS_EXIT` are head-carved at 6, so raise the head const temporarily to count real cycles), or a temporary unconditional `[DBG]` trace in `ipc::do_ipc` keyed on the caller nr (remove before committing)

- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless

- `cargo test -p minixrs-kernel` runs zero tests by design — every kernel module is gated on `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` and host-test infra is not yet built; host-runnable tests live in `kernel-shared`. QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)

content.text:
All four blocking gates pass. Slice 4.7 is complete and verified end-to-end.

## Summary

**Slice 4.7 — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary** is implemented on branch `feature/phase-4-7-exec`. I did **not** commit (per your auto-mode rule — surfacing for review first).

### What changed
- **Kernel `do_exec`** (`kernel/src/system/do_exec.rs`, new): `SYS_EXEC` becomes real and moves from the caller-local stub arm to the **target-taking** dispatch beside `SYS_FORK`. It resolves the binary by name (`module_by_name`), builds a fresh AS via a new `userland::load_exec_image` (factored out of `load_boot_server`), resets the target's frame, swaps `ttbr0_pa`/`asid`, reclaims the old image via `do_exit::teardown_addrspace` (now `pub(super)`), and unblocks the target at `_start`. `SELF`/self-target rejected; exec preserves pid/priv/scheduler; no reply on success.
- **kernel-shared**: `PM_EXEC = 0x704` (`NR_PM_MSGS` 4→5), `EXEC_NAME_LEN = 16`, `EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR = -1`, extended contiguity tests.
- **PM** (`servers/pm/src/main.rs`): `handle_exec` + `sys_exec` helper; hardcodes `EXEC_TARGET = "worker"` for the demo.
- **Worker** (`userland/worker/`, new crate): a freestanding getpid-loop + exit ELF — no SEF — packed into the MXBI archive by `build.rs` with proc_nr `-1` so the boot loader skips it (resolvable by name, never boot-loaded).
- **Demo**: stub E's child branch flips `PM_EXIT` → `PM_EXEC`.
- **Docs**: reconciled the stale **4.6b** marker to `✓ shipped (PR #29)`; wrote the 4.7 summary and slid `◀ next` to 4.8; added the CLAUDE.md convention note and the `userland/**/src/main.rs` coverage exclusion.

### Verification (QEMU, 25s)
6× `fork → child execs worker → worker exits → parent reaps → loop`, all reusing child slot 16 with advancing endpoint generation (`0x10→0x18010`) and recycled ASIDs — proof of exec teardown + reap + recycle. Worker absent from `[as]` (exec-only). All regressions intact (A↔B, C, D's faults+SIGSEGV, SCHED `[noq]`, 6 RS alarms). Zero panics; every `result=0`. Blocking gates: **fmt / clippy / test / kernel-aarch64 all PASS**.

Two things to flag before a PR, per your workflow:
1. I updated CLAUDE.md as part of the slice — happy to also run `/claude-md-management:revise-claude-md` if you'd like a separate audit pass.
2. Let me know if you have additional changes or notes to fold in, and I'll then commit + open the PR (regular merge, not squash).

cd /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs
gh pr create --base main --head feature/phase-4-7-exec \
  --title "phase 4.7: exec — SYS_EXEC + PM exec of a boot-embedded worker" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Slice 4.7 — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary

Adds the last missing piece of the process lifecycle: **exec**, replacing a
process's program image in place. A user proc `SENDREC`s `PM_EXEC` to PM; PM
issues `SYS_EXEC` naming that proc as the target; the kernel builds a fresh
address space, loads a boot-embedded ELF, resets the target to the new entry
point, and tears down the old address space. The exec target is a tiny
freestanding `worker` ELF packed into the MXBI archive.

### Kernel
- `kernel/src/system/do_exec.rs` (new): `SYS_EXEC` becomes real and moves from
  the caller-local stub arm to the **target-taking** dispatch beside `SYS_FORK`
  (PM names the exec-ing proc). Resolves the binary by name
  (`BootImage::module_by_name`), builds the AS via the new
  `userland::load_exec_image` (factored out of `load_boot_server`;
  partial-tree cleanup on OOM), resets the register frame, renames the proc,
  swaps `(ttbr0_pa, asid)`, reclaims the old image via
  `do_exit::teardown_addrspace` (now `pub(super)`), and `rts_unset(RTS_RECEIVING)`
  to resume at `_start`. `SELF`/self-target rejected (active-TTBR0 hazard); the
  target is gated like `do_fork`'s parent. exec preserves pid/priv/scheduler;
  **no reply on success**, errno reply on failure.
- `kernel-shared`: `PM_EXEC = 0x704` (`NR_PM_MSGS` 4→5), `EXEC_NAME_LEN = 16`,
  `EXEC_ONLY_PROC_NR = -1`, extended contiguity tests. `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4`
  unchanged (`SYS_EXEC` was already numbered `0x603` and granted to PM).

### PM & worker
- PM `handle_exec` issues `sys_exec(caller, EXEC_TARGET="worker")` — a
  user-supplied path arrives with Phase-5 musl/filesystem; the kernel path is
  already name-driven. No new priv wiring.
- `userland/worker` (new crate): freestanding getpid-loop + `PM_EXIT` ELF (no
  SEF). `build.rs` packs it with sentinel proc_nr `-1` so the boot loader
  **skips** it — resolvable by name, never boot-loaded.
- Demo: stub E's child branch flips `PM_EXIT` → `PM_EXEC`.

### Docs
- Reconciles the stale **4.6b** marker → `✓ shipped (PR #29)`; writes the 4.7
  summary and slides `◀ next` to 4.8; adds the CLAUDE.md convention note and the
  `userland/**/src/main.rs` coverage exclusion.

### Verification (QEMU, 25 s)
- Eleven `[as]` lines — **worker absent** (exec-only, skipped by the loader).
- Six `fork → child execs worker → worker exits → parent reaps → loop` cycles:
  `[ksys SYS_EXEC] target=16 name=worker` between matched `SYS_FORK`/`SYS_EXIT`
  `nr=16`, all reusing child slot 16 with a monotonically advancing endpoint
  generation (`0x10 → 0x18010`) and recycled ASIDs (exec teardown + reap proof).
- Worker `PM_GETPID` SENDRECs surface (`caller=16 target=0x0`).
- Regressions intact: A↔B ping-pong, C `SYS_GETINFO`, D's 3 `[pf]` + SIGSEGV
  kill, SCHED `[noq]`, six RS `[alarm]` fires.
- Zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`; every traced `result=0`.
- Host gates green: `cargo fmt --all --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace
  --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo kernel-aarch64`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

- **Commit**: GPG-signed (`G`), 19 files, +613/−72

Slice 4.7 is complete: `SYS_EXEC` is real and PM-driven, the `worker` binary execs into a forked child, and the full fork→exec→exit→reap→recycle loop is verified in QEMU with all prior-slice regressions intact. The stale 4.6b plan marker is reconciled to `✓ shipped (PR #29)` and `◀ next` now points at slice 4.8 (init).

Per your workflow, once CI is green and you merge (regular merge, not squash), I can do the local cleanup (`git checkout main && git pull`, delete the branch, prune) whenever you'd like.
